runners.name: Quiet Soul
runners.spotlight: Bred to develop into a capable sprinter but this might well be a stronger race than the one in which she ran second at Southwell (5f AW).
runners.comment: This might well be a stronger race than the one in which she ran second at Southwell

runners.name: Crystal Queen
runners.spotlight: Another breeze-up purchase who fetched 70,000gns; half-sister to 7f winner We've Got This (RPR 69); dam unraced half-sister to useful winners Riskit Fora Biskit (5f/6f including Listed), Crown Prosecutor (6f/7f) and Fox Trotter (6f/7f); the market should be enlightening.
runners.comment: Another breeze-up purchase who fetched 70,000gns; market should be enlightening

runners.name: Ruler's Pride
runners.spotlight: 550,000gns breeze-up buy; third foal; brother to 6f AW 2yo winner Naughty Eyes (RPR 76); dam placed 5f/6f AW (71), sister to 5f winner Suwaan, closely related to 6f Group 1 (Prix Morny and Commonwealth Cup) winner Campanelle; could very easily win this en route to something bigger.
runners.comment: 550,000gns breeze-up buy; could very easily win this en route to something bigger

runners.name: Classy Clarets
runners.spotlight: Showed a likeable attitude to get off the mark on turf at the 18th attempt at Hamilton (5f, good; Wee Mary fourth) on Friday evening; escapes a penalty for that and Mohammed Tabti is able to claim his 5lb allowance here; equally effective at 6f-7f; obvious chance.
runners.comment: Off the mark on turf at Hamilton (5f, good) on Friday; escapes a penalty; obvious chance.

runners.name: Summerstorms Dream
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo; was doing her best work in the closing stages when third over C&D (good to firm; 12-1) a fortnight ago; her trainer Jim Goldie saddles three runners with Lauren Young switching to Doon The Glen; consult the market.
runners.comment: Doing best work late when 3rd over C&D (12-1) fortnight ago; one of three for Jim Goldie.

runners.name: Doon The Glen
runners.spotlight: 1-30; that win a narrow one at Newcastle (6f) in March 2025; runner-up at that same venue (5f) two months ago but less productive at Thirsk and Hamilton since; the least likely of the three Jim Goldie-trained runners.
runners.comment: 1-30; runner-up at Newcastle (5f) three back; less productive at Thirsk & Hamilton since.

runners.name: Canaria Sun
runners.spotlight: Still seeking his first win (0-9); one of two to race on the far side when third at Redcar (6f, good; 5-1) a fortnight ago; should find a winning opportunity eventually but there are at least a couple of others who make more appeal here.
runners.comment: 0-9; third at Redcar (6f, good; 5-1) fortnight ago; a few of the others make more appeal.

runners.name: Bernie The Bear
runners.spotlight: Yet to win on turf (0-17) and his 'most recent' AW win was at Wolverhampton (6f) in December 2023; nothing positive to take from his last four starts, including when weakening quickly at Musselburgh (7f; 40-1) three weeks ago.
runners.comment: 0-17 on turf; nothing positive to take from his last four starts; isn't the solution.

runners.name: Siviez
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who has dropped 11lb in the weights since introduced to handicapping; attracted market support when a creditable fourth to Irish Dancer at Catterick (5f, good) last week; return to 6f should suit; dangerous to dismiss.
runners.comment: Creditable fourth to Irish Dancer at Catterick (5f) last week; return to 6f should suit.

runners.name: A Lady Forever
runners.spotlight: With Roger Fell when winning a weak 0-50 classified at Newcastle (6f; 20-1) in February 2025; no joy since for Kieran Burke (0-3) or Gary Harrison (0-11); weakened from the two-furlong pole at Redcar (6f; eighth of 11) latest; new blinkers today; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Sole win in weak 0-50 classified at Newcastle in February 2025; 0-14 since; look elsewhere

runners.name: Wee Mary
runners.spotlight: 1-22; third to a stablemate of Classy Clarets over 5f here (12-1) on penultimate start, and better than the bare form suggests - switched left to make solid late headway - when fourth to Classy Clarets at Hamilton (5f apprentices') on Friday evening; 6f looks ideal; player.
runners.comment: Late headway - fourth to Classy Clarets - at Hamilton (5f) on Friday eve; 6f ideal; player

runners.name: Jet Warrior
runners.spotlight: Ex Irish; heavily supported and fared better in newly applied cheekpieces (now discarded) at Southwell (7f; 9-2, third of 11) a fortnight ago; drops back in trip for this return to turf; place chance.
runners.comment: Better in newly applied cheekpieces (now discarded) at Southwell (7f) latest; same mark.

runners.name: Goal Line
runners.spotlight: Placed on debut in a C&D maiden (good to soft) in August 2024, but low key on final two starts for Adrian Keatley; always behind, off a 506-day absence, on recent stable/handicap debut at Newcastle (6f); now 3lb lower.
runners.comment: Always behind, off a 506-day absence, on recent stable/handicap debut at Newcastle (6f).

runners.name: Pearl Eye
runners.spotlight: Won six for Darryll Holland, most notably when awarded the Spring Mile in March 2025; lost his way thereafter, and Gay Kelleway (0-4) couldn't rejuvenate; joined Jim Goldie for 13,000gns and there were positive signs when third over 1m here (stable debut) recently; is dangerously handicapped.
runners.comment: Positive signs when third over 1m here (stable debut) recently; is dangerously handicapped

runners.name: Jujubella
runners.spotlight: Three-time Newcastle winner who made the breakthrough on turf at Haydock (1m3f, good) last July; acquitted herself respectably in Class 2 contests at York (fifth) and Doncaster (seventh) last autumn, but her form has tapered off; fast easing handicap mark.
runners.comment: Respectable efforts in Class 2 contests at York & Doncaster last autumn; more modest since

runners.name: Motawaared
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in maiden/novice company for George Boughey, none more so than when runner-up to Michael Scofield at Southwell (1m) in March 2025; unconvincing over 1m2f-1m4f, and not yet threatened to click for Jim Goldie (0-2 since 12,000gns purchase); now on career-low mark.
runners.comment: Not yet threatened to click for J. Goldie (0-2 since 12,000gns purchase); career-low mark.

runners.name: Dingwall
runners.spotlight: Form figures 22362222 since justifying favouritism at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Class 6) last November; 1lb higher than when runner-up over C&D (6-1, slowly away) 15 days ago; form dipped at Wolverhampton on Monday.
runners.comment: Frustrating form figures; runner-up over C&D recently (now 1lb higher); below best Monday

runners.name: Ayr Poet
runners.spotlight: Rounded off last season with a game win here (1m Class 5, good to soft; from 4lb lower), that his 13th win; struggled in a competitive Sunday Series Handicap at Musselburgh 24 days ago, and it might be a similar story here; stablemate of Pearl Eye and Motawaared.
runners.comment: Struggled in competitive Sunday Series Handicap at Musselburgh 24 days ago; similar story?
runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing

runners.name: Footwork
runners.spotlight: Hit hard by the handicapper after winning at Haydock (1m, good to firm) last May; ran well fresh when third at Kempton (1m, Class 4) in October but held in better quality races since, including at Newmarket 18 days ago; moves beyond a mile for the first time.
runners.comment: Ran well when third at Kempton (1m, Class 4) in Oct; held in better quality races since.

runners.name: Candonomore
runners.spotlight: Justified favouritism at Bath (1m, firm) on final start for Stuart Williams; sold for 35,000gns since, and begins life for new connections from a 5lb higher mark; has undertaken most of his racing over 7f-1m but does stay (runner-up over 1m2f last June); claims, even if career-best is required.
runners.comment: Justified favouritism at Bath (1m, firm) final start for S. Williams; sold for 35,000gns.

runners.name: Livonian
runners.spotlight: Won once in four for Ralph Beckett, easily at short odds at Windsor (1m, novice; good) last September; underwhelming thereafter at Newcastle (1m) and Ripon (1m2f), and moved on for a knockdown 14,000gns; low mileage but best watched on stable debut (second run in a handicap).
runners.comment: Novice winner at Windsor (1m) last September; modest since; left R. Beckett for 14,000gns.

runners.name: Northwest Passage
runners.spotlight: Won by 15l at Salisbury (1m2f, heavy; 5-2 fav; first run as a gelding) last September, and posted another solid effort at Newbury (1m2f, soft) next time; now 14lb above that winning mark and seemed to need recent Musselburgh comeback run/stable debut (31,000gns buy from Roger Teal).
runners.comment: 14lb above winning mark; seemed to need recent Musselburgh comeback run/stable debut.

runners.name: Cisco Disco
runners.spotlight: Hit form with vengeance through the second half of 2025, winning four of his last five (all here, 1m-1m2f); the bulk of those were on soft to heavy, and he has risen a combined total of 23lb in the handicap; merits respect, but is returning from a 223-day absence; rain would help.
runners.comment: Won four of his last five here (1m-1m2f; rising 23lb in handicap); respected off a break.

runners.name: Good Morning Alex
runners.spotlight: 7-24 with his last win coming when joined on the line at Ripon (1m, good; 3-1 favourite) in April 2025; struggled on AW through the winter (0-4), and was pestered for the lead, weakening to finish fifth (beaten 8l), at Ripon five weeks ago; an easing mark isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Pestered for lead, weakening to finish fifth (btn 8l), at Ripon five weeks ago; down 3lb.

runners.name: Military Girl
runners.spotlight: 2-3 at Catterick, fending off Lucius Aurelius there (5f, good to firm; from 4lb higher) in April 2025; 0-8 since but did take a step back in the right direction when third to Betweenthesticks on seasonal reappearance at Pontefract (5f, good; 9-2) four weeks ago; possible player.
runners.comment: Step back in the right direction when third on seasonal reappearance at Pontefract (5f).

runners.name: Henery Hawk
runners.spotlight: Hit the line strongly at Wolverhampton (5f; from 6lb lower) over three weeks ago; he's 7-39 on AW, but is yet to win on turf (0-20); handicap mark is still a fair one, particularly with Tom Kiely-Marshall taking off 5lb, and this is the right type of race; don't discount.
runners.comment: Hit the line strongly at Wolverhampton (5f; 6lb lower) latest; yet to win on turf (0-20).

runners.name: Thunderstorm Katie
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (6f) who also notched back-to-back victories at Musselburgh and Newcastle (both 5f) last October; ran her best race since - flashed home after fluffing the start - at Hamilton on Friday evening; sits 1lb lower, and also has the excellent Lauren Young claiming 5lb.
runners.comment: Flashed home after fluffing start at Hamilton (5f) on Friday eve; 1lb lower; key player.

runners.name: Donald
runners.spotlight: Shaped encouragingly on seasonal/handicap debut when fourth at Nottingham (5f, good; 10-1) last week; it's still early days in his career, and he remains open to further improvement.
runners.comment: Fourth on handicap debut at Nottingham (5f, good; 10-1) last week; it's still early days.

runners.name: Jm Jhingree
runners.spotlight: Ex Jim Goldie, now 2lb below the mark he won from at Newcastle (5f; made all) in December; soundly beaten in two starts for this trainer, left trailing (last of eight) behind Woohoo at Musselburgh (5f; 40-1) three weeks ago; revival necessary.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in two starts for this trainer, including at Musselburgh (5f; 40-1) latest.

runners.name: Classy Al
runners.spotlight: No stranger to the Ayr winners' enclosure, winning five at this track (5f-7f), including this race two years ago; has struggled for form in two 5f spins at Musselburgh this year, which makes him hard to fancy; on the plus side, the handicapper is beginning to give him a chance.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2024; has struggled for form in two 5f spins at Musselburgh this year.

runners.name: Sir Paul Ramsey
runners.spotlight: Showed some spark on AW earlier this spring and he's 3lb lower than when winning at Haydock (1m, good) last August; not written off but he's been below par on turf on his last two starts and has to raise his game.
runners.comment: He's 3lb below last August's last winning mark but hasn't shone on turf on last two starts

runners.name: Hale End
runners.spotlight: Returned from two-month break with a win at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last month and he went close at Thirsk (1m, good) on Saturday; this C&D winner has very solid claims off the same mark as four days ago.
runners.comment: Went close at Thirsk on Saturday & this C&D winner has very solid claims off the same mark

runners.name: Penelope's Sister
runners.spotlight: Three C&D wins last year (various ground) and she's just 1lb above her last winning mark; soundly beaten over C&D a fortnight ago but she may have needed that reappearance outing and is not discounted.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins last year and she may be able to leave her reappearance run miles behind

runners.name: Priapos
runners.spotlight: Rejuvenated on AW late last year, winning two in a row at Newcastle (1m); it's possible he'll improve for last month's outing at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm; seventh of ten), which was his first of the year, but he needs to prove he can transfer his AW resurgence back to turf.
runners.comment: Won 2 in a row at Newcastle in late 2025; has to transfer that AW resurgence back to turf

runners.name: Jannas Journey
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Musselburgh (where she won last September) and Ascot on her three starts this season; she needs to get back on track but seven visits to Ayr have yielded five wins and the return to this track may well prompt a revival.
runners.comment: 5-7 at Ayr and the return to this track may well prompt a revival

runners.name: Kaleido
runners.spotlight: Ran five times for the Gosdens, winning a maiden at Yarmouth (7f, good) last July; no threat when sixth of ten at 9-1 on last month's stable and seasonal debut at Epsom (8.5f, good) but this well-bred 4yo may have needed that run and is just the type his new trainer does very well with; a possible.
runners.comment: May have needed his stable debut and he's just the type his new yard does very well with

runners.name: Degale
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who went close on handicap debut at Haydock (1m, good to firm) last August and, after three lesser runs on AW, was back on song when 1l third at Nottingham (8.3f, good) 11 days ago; this front-runner can make another bold bid and could be bang there once more.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo who was a close third at Nottingham recently when back on turf

runners.name: Mr Mistoffelees
runners.spotlight: Often ran well last year, including on turf, and won at Newcastle (1m, AW) in October; however, he's finished down the field on both outings this season; he may be of greater interest when the blinkers return.
runners.comment: Often ran well last year but he's finished down the field on both outings this season

runners.name: Geo
runners.spotlight: With a new yard this year; her mark continues to slide and Jim Goldie should find the key at some stage, but she needs to produce more than she's managed on her first three starts for the stable.
runners.comment: Her mark continues to slide but she needs to improve upon this season's form

runners.name: Redarna
runners.spotlight: 18 visits to Ayr have yielded nine wins, the latest last September; the return to Ayr could be the catalyst for a big run but he's struggled at Musselburgh and Thirsk this year and a major turnaround is needed from this 12yo.
runners.comment: Nine-time course winner but he's struggled at Musselburgh and Thirsk this year

runners.name: Return To Unit
runners.spotlight: Made debut last September and improved with each of his first three runs, winning at Nottingham (8.3f, good) in October when last seen; Newmarket trainer Roger Varian has a very healthy 29% strike-rate here and this well-bred 4yo has considerable potential off his opening mark.
runners.comment: Well-bred Newmarket challenger who has considerable potential off his opening mark

runners.name: Garden Oasis
runners.spotlight: 11yo who won three times last year, including this race (good to firm); this front-runner has been placed at Pontefract (1m, good) and Ripon (1m2f, good) on his last two starts; 3-5 at Ayr and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: 11yo who won this last year and arrives in good form; 3-5 at Ayr and he's on the shortlist

runners.name: Kelpie Grey
runners.spotlight: Went off the boil towards the end of last season and also below his best on last month's reappearance at Musselburgh, taking his losing sequence to 15; however, this two-time C&D winner may have needed that latest outing and he's well handicapped on last year's best course form; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Return to form needed but he's well handicapped on last year's best efforts here

runners.name: Royal Blaze
runners.spotlight: His two wins last August/September (including here) came over 1m2f but he travelled well when fourth over 1m1f here (good to firm) on his recent return to action, when short of room close home, and he may have versatility in terms of trip; interesting contender.
runners.comment: His last two wins (including here) were over 1m2f but he may be versatile trip-wise

runners.name: Novak
runners.spotlight: Beaten 13 times since last win at Newcastle (7f, AW) last September but close fifth of seven at Ayr (1m, good to firm) recently and he may appreciate the return to 7f; his mark continues to fall and he might not be far away.
runners.comment: Close fifth of seven over 1m at Ayr recently and he may appreciate the return to 7f

runners.name: Roaring Ralph
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row at Southwell (1m, AW) earlier this year and solid second there (1m) a fortnight ago; he won here in 2024 and holds strong claims if transferring his fine recent form back to turf.
runners.comment: AW hat-trick this year; C&D win in 2024; strong claims if fine form continues back on turf

runners.name: Elizabeth Bay
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has shown ability and has edged down the weights, but she wasn't at the top of her game on her final three runs last season and remains a maiden after ten starts; others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Has edged down the weights but is 0-10 and others are more compelling; makes reappearance

runners.name: Valentine Catcher
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent sort who was soundly beaten at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last time and his losing run continues; however, he kept on well for second at Pontefract (6f, good) two starts ago and is not written off.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at Doncaster latest but good run at Pontefract previously; not written off

runners.name: Maui Breeze
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Newcastle (1m, AW) in December on second stable start and she's also effective on turf; however, she was down the field at Newcastle (1m/1m2f, AW) on her last three starts and returns from a break today.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Newcastle in December but down the field there on her three starts since
runners.owner: H S Hutchinson And W Glass

runners.name: Al Muqdad
runners.spotlight: His form has been patchy since an easy win at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last November but he's 2lb below that last winning mark and his form figures at Ayr read 3112, so it will be no surprise if he's in the mix.
runners.comment: 2lb below last winning mark and form figures at Ayr read 3112; could be in the mix

runners.name: Mr Cool
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent and soundly beaten back on turf at Doncaster this month; however, this two-time turf winner was runner-up at Kempton (7f, AW) two starts ago and is not discounted off a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: Inconsistent and soundly beaten last time but runner-up previously and not discounted

runners.name: Qazaq
runners.spotlight: 0-15 since winning his first three starts; however, he went close at Musselburgh (7f, good) two starts ago despite trouble in running and was again denied a clear run when sixth there (7f, good) last time; could be in the mix if getting the breaks.
runners.comment: Trouble in running at Musselburgh on last two starts, going close on the first occasion

runners.name: Pension Pot
runners.spotlight: Faded into 3l fifth of nine on handicap/seasonal debut at Nottingham last month and this headstrong sort also looked stretched by 1m at Thirsk last time; the return to 7f looks the right move for this lightly raced 4yo, who is a possible player.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo for whom the return to 7f looks a positive move; possible player

runners.name: Great
runners.spotlight: Not at the top of his game this season but hasn't run badly over 6f on his last two starts and he's possibly better at 7f; he's dropped down the weights and could have a big part to play.
runners.comment: On a reduced mark and the return to 7f may also be a positive; could have big part to play

runners.name: Habrdi
runners.spotlight: He has an up-and-down profile but he won at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in March and finished second of seven over this C&D (good to firm) a fortnight ago; leading claims if backing up that latest performance.
runners.comment: Up-and-down profile but second over C&D recently and leading claims if backing that up
runners.owner: Mrs S D Pearson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Native Honey
runners.spotlight: Down the field over about 1m at Musselburgh and Hamilton on his two starts this season; however, his sole win came over this C&D last August and it's possible the return to Ayr and drop back to 7f will trigger a revival.
runners.comment: Down the field elsewhere on his two starts this season but his sole win came over C&D

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: Some good form this spring prior to winning at Musselburgh (7f, good) last month; flopped at Leicester (7f, good to firm) 11 days ago but he shouldn't be written off today.
runners.comment: Won at Musselburgh last month; flopped at Leicester 11 days ago but he's not written off

runners.name: Macedonian
runners.spotlight: Just one win from his 15 starts and he's inconsistent; however, he could improve for last month's reappearance run at Thirsk and he's on a handy mark on last season's best 6f form; has hinted a few times that this step back up to 7f will be worth exploring and he's not discounted.
runners.comment: Just 1-15 and inconsistent but on a handy mark and the return to 7f is worth exploring

runners.name: Golden Valour
runners.spotlight: 10yo who made a winning reappearance here in 2024 (sole course win from 29 attempts) and was on the scoresheet at Musselburgh last September; however, his campaign petered out tamely and a return to form is needed on his seasonal debut.
runners.comment: 10yo for whom a return to form is necessary on his seasonal debut

runners.name: Rwenearlytheredad
runners.spotlight: His form went the wrong way after he made a winning reappearance at Carlisle last May but he returns having had wind surgery and the break may have helped; 2lb lower than when last successful and could be involved.
runners.comment: Form went wrong way last season after winning reappearance, but returns after wind op

runners.name: Wolf Of Badenoch
runners.spotlight: Very smart 2yo who has lost his way but off a much reduced mark he was a close fourth at Doncaster (1m, good to firm) after being well backed; he might well have won that race on another day as he hit some traffic.
runners.comment: Heavily backed at Doncaster last time and he might well have won on another day

runners.name: Glorious Kitty
runners.spotlight: Now 0-14; bang there at Beverley but had no excuses when well positioned at Musselburgh (1m1f, good); might be vulnerable.
runners.comment: Had no excuses when well positioned at Musselburgh (1m1f, good); might be vulnerable

runners.name: South Road
runners.spotlight: 40-1 winner of a 6f handicap here last summer; well below that level in her last three races (6f/7f) and there are stamina doubts over this far.
runners.comment: 40-1 winner of a 6f handicap here last summer; there are stamina doubts over this far

runners.name: Mwafaq
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick when narrowly prevailing at Thirsk (7f, good) and his chance was compromised at Southwell by a wide trip; remains of interest, and he stays 1m.
runners.comment: Chance was compromised at Southwell by a wide trip; remains of interest
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Evelyn's Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Won on his Nottingham reappearance (8.3f, good) last month and was then second at Leicester; possibilities on those two efforts but latest Beverley run was underwhelming.
runners.comment: Capable at this level but his latest Beverley run was underwhelming

runners.name: Tap Dancer
runners.spotlight: Won a Bath novice when with Marcus Tregoning whom she left for 8,000gns; only seventh on recent handicap debut here over 1m.
runners.comment: Bath winner but only seventh on recent handicap debut here over 1m

runners.name: Lovette
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner last July (1m, good); no closer than about 3l in her six handicaps and finished last here two weeks ago.
runners.comment: No closer than about 3l in her six handicaps and finished last here two weeks ago

runners.name: Sophiesticate
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly at longer trips (stays 12.5f) and offered little back over 1m at Newcastle in early December.
runners.comment: Stays further and offered little back over 1m at Newcastle in early December

races.race_name: Hazelton Mountford Insurance Brokers Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Rebel Tribesman
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper at Aintree last May; changed hands subsequently and has just been touched off when a short-priced favourite in a couple of Sedgefield maidens over 2m1f since switching to hurdles; steps up in trip and looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Just touched off when a short-priced favourite in a couple of Sedgefield maidens.

runners.name: Mr Rafiki
runners.spotlight: Last of four in a Ludlow bumper in October; had wind surgery subsequently and stayed on well when fourth of ten finishers in a Ffos Las maiden (2m4f, good to soft) last month on hurdles debut; that was a step in the right direction but more is required here.
runners.comment: Stayed on well when fourth in a Ffos Las maiden last month on hurdles debut.

runners.name: Royal Rocket
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on his hurdles debut at Chepstow to finish third of seven in a Taunton novice (2m3f, good) last month; is going the right way and holds claims, but his best chance of success will be in handicaps.
runners.comment: Stepped up on his hurdles debut to finish third in a Taunton novice last month.

runners.name: Bathara
runners.spotlight: Landed bumper around here last October but has cut little ice in his two starts over hurdles; this looks easier but he needs to start producing.
runners.comment: Landed bumper here last October but has cut little ice in his two starts over hurdles.

runners.name: The Craftymaster
runners.spotlight: A nine-time Flat winner over 1m6f-2m, he made a pleasing hurdles debut when third of five finishers (with stablemate Alex The Great 27l back) in a Hereford maiden (2m, good) 15 days ago; steps up in trip and holds place claims.
runners.comment: Made a pleasing hurdles debut when third in a Hereford maiden 15 days ago.
runners.owner: R Miles, V Miles, T Stamp & Partner

runners.name: Blue's Secret Gem
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when in the frame in a couple of bumpers but finished last of seven on hurdles debut at Fakenham (2m4f, good) 16 days ago; may not come into her own until she is qualified for handicaps.
runners.comment: Finished last of seven on hurdles debut at Fakenham 16 days ago.

runners.name: Madmoiselle Moss
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a couple of bumpers and on hurdles debut last month.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a couple of bumpers and on hurdles debut last month.

runners.name: Alex The Great
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner at a modest level on Flat; beaten long way in two similar events over 2m at Hereford recently; steps up in trip but easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: Beaten long way in two similar events over 2m at Hereford recently.

runners.name: Edgewell
runners.spotlight: Landed back-to-back wins at Hereford over 3m1f on good this spring; found out in a higher grade twice since but back at his winning level and should run his race.
runners.comment: Landed back-to-back wins at Hereford over 3m1f on good this spring.

runners.name: Tom Desjy
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on his third start over fences at Southwell (3m, good) nine days ago; carries a 7lb penalty but this lightly raced type has scope for further improvement and will be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Got off the mark on his third start over fences at Southwell nine days ago.

runners.name: Grand Sabre
runners.spotlight: His sole success came here (3m, good) last May off this mark but he's been disappointing since; first-time cheekpieces are applied on his first run for five months and they need to spark him back to form.
runners.comment: His sole success came here last May off this mark; has been disappointing since.

runners.name: Yes And Yes
runners.spotlight: Appreciated the step up in trip when landing his second race over fences at Stratford (3m3f, good) last month; up 5lb and needs to back that up over this slightly shorter distance.
runners.comment: Stepped up in trip when landing his second race over fences at Stratford last month.
runners.owner: David W Jagger & Mrs Belinda Clarke

runners.name: Testflight
runners.spotlight: 1-28 under rules, his win coming over fences off this mark at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) last July; has been off since October and needs to hit the ground running.
runners.comment: 1-28 under rules, his win coming over fences off this mark at Newton Abbot last July.

runners.name: John W Creasy
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner on slow ground (2m/2m3f) but out of sorts last term and has a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner on slow ground but out of sorts last term and has a lot to prove.

runners.name: The Moonlight Man
runners.spotlight: Point winner who showed his first form over fences when second of three in a weak contest at Ffos Las (3m, heavy) in March; had excuses when pulled up last time but does need to show improvement.
runners.comment: Showed his first form over fences when second in a weak contest at Ffos Las in March.

races.race_name: Mane Event: Logicor Cup Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Full Force Gale
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who has shown significant improvement since fitted with a tongue-tie and cheekpieces in handicaps over fences, winning over C&D (good) 16 days ago; further progress looks nailed on and he should be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Has shown significant improvement in handicaps over fences, winning over C&D 16 days ago.

runners.name: King Of The Story
runners.spotlight: Has four wins over fences, including over C&D; encouraging fourth at Fontwell (2m3f, good) on his return last month following a five-month break but he's 2lb higher than his last winning mark.
runners.comment: Fourth at Fontwell on his return last month following a five-month break.

runners.name: Rangatira Jack
runners.spotlight: Sole success came at Exeter off 7lb higher in April last year; has slipped down the weights but placed for the first time since when second of six finishers at Ffos Las (2m5f, good) 15 days ago; cannot dismiss.
runners.comment: Sole success came at Exeter in April last year; second at Ffos Las 15 days ago.
runners.owner: Mrs D Robinson Mrs & Mrs Livesey-Van Dorst

runners.name: King Of The Road
runners.spotlight: Won three races in 2024; inconsistent since but ran well when second at Kempton (2m5f, good) on Boxing Day; two poor runs since but has undergone wind surgery and a first-time tongue-tie replaces cheekpieces; interesting if the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Ran well when second at Kempton on Boxing Day, before two poor runs; wind operation since

runners.name: Crest Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who landed his second race over hurdles at Taunton (2m3f, good) last month; shapes as though he'll be suited by this step up in distance; respected.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who landed his second race over hurdles at Taunton last month.

runners.name: Il Va De Soi
runners.spotlight: Put a disappointing run of form behind him when returning to winning ways over C&D (good) 11 days ago, making all; whether he can reproduce that effort is another matter but he is still well handicapped on his old form.
runners.comment: Put a disappointing run of form behind him when winning over C&D 11 days ago, making all.

runners.name: La Vish
runners.spotlight: Showed his first worthwhile form when scoring over C&D at 20-1 in a three-runner contest in March; showed that to be no fluke when third of five at Perth (2m4f, good) on his handicap debut last month and not out of this.
runners.comment: 20-1 winner (first form) in three-runner C&D race in March; backed that up with Perth 3rd.

runners.name: Jimmy Hurdstrom
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner yet to get off the mark under rules; ran his best race when third over C&D in March; below that form when a warm favourite in a Ffos Las maiden last month but may warrant another chance.
runners.comment: Below form in a Ffos Las maiden last month but may warrant another chance.

runners.name: Champagne Twist
runners.spotlight: 2024 EBF Final winner off 7lb higher; unseated too far out at Uttoxeter latest to say if and where he may have figured, but his poor winter up to that point tempers enthusiasm either way; overlooked.
runners.comment: 2024 EBF Final winner off 7lb higher but has disappointed since.

runners.name: Norman Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Has had three wins over hurdles but yet to convince on his suitability for this distance, latest when seventh of eight finishers at Haydock (2m5f, good) last month in first-time cheekpieces (retained).
runners.comment: Has had three wins over hurdles but yet to convince on his suitability for this distance.

races.race_name: Cor Blimey Gallop Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Allibaba
runners.spotlight: Made light of a 538-day absence to win for the first time on his stable debut at Market Rasen (2m, good) 12 days ago; handicapper has reacted by raising him 9lb but that may not be enough to stop him following up.
runners.comment: Made light of a 538-day absence to win for the first time at Market Rasen 12 days ago.

runners.name: Westerton
runners.spotlight: Sole success came on the Flat in a Sandown maiden in 2023 when trained by Alan King; beaten 29l on stable debut at Uttoxeter (2m, good) 18 days ago in his first handicap hurdle; best watched.
runners.comment: Beaten 29l on stable debut at Uttoxeter 18 days ago.
runners.owner: A Baxter, S, Sowray, K Ward

runners.name: Louie The Legend
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat winner; ran his best race in this sphere when third of ten finishers at Southwell (2m, good) nine days ago on his handicap debut; has scope to improve further and should run well.
runners.comment: Ran his best race in this sphere when third at Southwell nine days ago.

runners.name: Onehundredneighty
runners.spotlight: Showed modest form for Max Young, including in two handicaps; it will be interesting to see if the switch to this major stable can eke out any improvement; betting may well be significant.
runners.comment: Interesting to see if the switch to this major stable can eke out any improvement.

runners.name: Golly Gosh
runners.spotlight: Modest form in five runs over hurdles, pulled up in handicaps on last two; first-time tongue-tie worn last time is persevered with, joined by first-time blinkers; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Modest form in five runs over hurdles, pulled up in handicaps on last two; blinkers now.

runners.name: Buxted Reel
runners.spotlight: All three wins under rules on AW Flat; returns from a break and best watched unless the market speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Returns from a break and best watched unless the market speaks favourably.

runners.name: Karthala
runners.spotlight: Has shown little in three runs over hurdles; makes his handicap hurdle debut and the cheekpieces he wore on his most recent Flat starts return; best to check for market confidence.
runners.comment: Has shown in little in three runs over hurdles; makes his handicap hurdle debut.

runners.name: Babychino
runners.spotlight: Has shown modest form (best penultimate start) and needs improvement.
runners.comment: Has shown modest form (best penultimate start) and needs improvement.

runners.name: Brendas Asking
runners.spotlight: Both wins have came at Ludlow when claimer ridden; ran respectably following 12 months off when third of seven at Haydock (1m7f, good) last month; all of her best form previously had been over right-handed tracks, so a question remains over her suitability for this course.
runners.comment: Ran respectably following 12 months off when third of seven at Haydock last month.
runners.owner: Dr David Carey (Not The Turf Club)

runners.name: Scintillante
runners.spotlight: Won twice at Worcester last summer but below his best on his last two starts and needs to come down a few pounds.
runners.comment: Won twice at Worcester last summer but below his best on his last two starts.

runners.name: Mojito Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Won in France in March 2025; an eye-catcher on his fourth start in Britain when beaten a neck at Ascot (1m7f, good) in March, on his handicap debut following wind surgery; has scope for further improvement and should be hard to beat; he is Harry Cobden's only booked ride of the meeting.
runners.comment: An eye-catcher last time on fourth start in Britain when beaten a neck at Ascot in March.

runners.name: Chief Sunday
runners.spotlight: Dual winner in this sphere but has been operating below his best for a while and is best left alone at present.
runners.comment: Dual winner in this sphere but has been operating below his best for a while.

runners.name: Bluegrass
runners.spotlight: Has five wins over hurdles but below form over further on his last two starts; looks to be in the handicapper's grip at present.
runners.comment: Has five wins over hurdles but below form over further on his last two starts.

runners.name: High Fibre
runners.spotlight: At his best as a hurdler when gaining his second win in this sphere, at Kempton (2m, good) in March; touched off at Ludlow next time but below his best up in trip at Plumpton (2m4f, good) last month; ridden on four of last five starts by Ciaran O'Shea; back to 2m and needs to return to form.
runners.comment: Second win in this sphere came at Kempton in March; below form latest

runners.name: Kingston Sunflower
runners.spotlight: Has a strike rate of 5-12 over hurdles but hasn't been seen since a below-par third at Ludlow in December; needs to raise her game to new heights having never won off a mark this high, but still commands respect.
runners.comment: Hasn't been seen since a below-par third at Ludlow in December; still respected.

runners.name: Unjeu Royal
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who got off the mark in this sphere on his handicap debut at Plumpton (2m, good) last month; scrambled home that day but is lightly raced and may show further improvement off 5lb higher.
runners.comment: Got off the mark in this sphere on his handicap debut at Plumpton last month.

runners.name: Forget The Way
runners.spotlight: In decent form when last seen in November, winning a seller at Huntingdon and not beaten far in a better race there subsequently; this looks a competitive race for his return to action but he is not discounted.
runners.comment: Won at Huntingdon in November and not beaten far in a better race there subsequently.

races.race_name: Mortal Bunny Rum Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: October Hill
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare who is 0-11 under rules and has been beaten about 15l in her two handicaps; unwise to rate her subsequent placed form in maidens as improvement; cheekpieces tried last time are removed.
runners.comment: 0-11 under rules and has been beaten about 15l in her two handicaps

runners.name: Aslukgoes
runners.spotlight: Good bumper form (three wins, including Listed) and a novice hurdle winner (all on good); nowhere near that level in two handicaps for current yard and cheekpieces are now turned to; the prospect of softening ground is a further concern.
runners.comment: Useful at best but quiet for current yard and softening ground would be a concern

runners.name: Lemon Leaf
runners.spotlight: They only jumped four hurdles when this lightly raced 6yo made a successful handicap debut over C&D, winning by just under 4l; took a 10lb hit for that but he looks progressive.
runners.comment: Took a 10lb hit for recent C&D success but he looks progressive

runners.name: Lion Of The Desert
runners.spotlight: Back over this trip he's gone back-to-back in course handicaps (good ground; placed on soft), last time nicely on top in the finish; up another 5lb but looks sure to be involved.
runners.comment: Back over this trip he's gone back-to-back in course handicaps; up 5lb for last time

runners.name: Red Rubio
runners.spotlight: 3m point winner; from off the pace he was an encouraging third on handicap debut at Hereford (2m, good) and this longer trip looks sure to benefit him; considered.
runners.comment: From off the pace he was an encouraging third on handicap debut; raised in distance

races.race_name: Happy 1st Birthday Donnacha Fenelon Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: One Knight
runners.spotlight: Won his first two bumpers for this yard under Cameron Johnstone-Baker (good to soft/good) before disappointing in a Grade 2 at the Grand National meeting; could easily be up to this on his hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Dual bumper winner who could easily be up to this on his hurdling debut

runners.name: Fiskardo
runners.spotlight: Back from a long absence to edge home in a good-ground bumper at Perth last month (first run in a tongue-tie); others in here did have superior form in that scene.
runners.comment: Bumper winner last month but others in here did have superior form in that scene

runners.name: The Flaggy Shore
runners.spotlight: Point winner who won a Warwick bumper by 7l and contested two Listed races since then, finishing second on the first occasion; promising recruit to hurdling.
runners.comment: Good enough in bumpers to appeal as a promising recruit to hurdling

runners.name: Brenda Lady
runners.spotlight: Eleventh foal; sister to bumper/2m5f hurdle winner Far Sight, half-sister to five winners including Dare To Endeavour (useful 2m4f-3m chase), Holly James (bumper/2m hurdle) and Grand Enterprise (2m2f/3m hurdle); dam 2m-2m5f hurdle winner; appealing pedigree but this is a belated racecourse debut.
runners.comment: Appealing pedigree but this is a belated racecourse debut

runners.name: Kenobi
runners.spotlight: Pulled up or tailed off in his three runs over hurdles and makes very little appeal.
runners.comment: Pulled up or tailed off in his three runs over hurdles and holds no appeal

races.race_name: Happy 1st Birthday Idris Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Green Asset
runners.spotlight: An improved clear second over 2m5f at Newton Abbot last month and he looks ready for this longer trip; should run well.
runners.comment: Improved second over 2m5f at Newton Abbot and he looks ready for this longer trip

runners.name: Glenmuick
runners.spotlight: His first run at 3m and in cheekpieces when a rallying second here a month ago (good); may well have more to offer.
runners.comment: His first run at 3m and in cheekpieces when a rallying second here a month ago

runners.name: Poet's Muse
runners.spotlight: Point winner; first worthwhile run under rules when beaten a head on her handicap debut at Taunton (3m, good) and that's something to build on; went up 2lb and Paddy Hanlon takes off 5lb this afternoon, so she's weighted to have a say.
runners.comment: First worthwhile run under rules when beaten a head on her handicap debut at Taunton

runners.name: Blackacre
runners.spotlight: In a new visor he won two 3m2f handicap chases at Newton Abbot last August; has looked too high in the weights since then and he's 0-11 as a hurdler; others preferred.
runners.comment: Two chase wins last August but hasn't looked well treated since then; 0-11 as a hurdler

runners.name: Good Girl Rachel
runners.spotlight: Beaten 6l in one of her points; pretty much tailed off in her three maiden/novice hurdles (2m-2m3f) but this longer trip is probably a good move for her handicap debut; tries cheekpieces.
runners.comment: This longer trip is probably a good move for her handicap debut; tries cheekpieces

runners.name: Hard Stare
runners.spotlight: Some promise in bumpers but has yet to get anything going over hurdles and was beaten 25l on his handicap debut at Taunton (3m, good; 6-1).
runners.comment: Yet to get anything going over hurdles and was beaten 25l on his handicap debut

runners.name: Alderley Charlie
runners.spotlight: Form figures of PFPP over fences and, with a tongue-tie added, he never counted on his return to hurdles at Exeter; opposable.
runners.comment: Never took to fences and never counted on his return to hurdles at Exeter; opposable

runners.name: Crest Of Valour
runners.spotlight: It's been underwhelming stuff thus far but he's treated accordingly for this handicap debut and a tongue-tie is added; entitled to show more.
runners.comment: Quiet so far but now makes handicap debut with a tongue-tie added

races.race_name: Francky Du Berlais Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Loki's Mischief
runners.spotlight: Placed over hurdles; only his second run over fences when doing it fairly comfortably here two weeks ago (2m5f, good; fine on softer) and he promises to stay this far; every chance off a 6lb higher mark.
runners.comment: His second run over fences when doing it fairly comfortably here two weeks ago (2m5f)

runners.name: Jack's Jury
runners.spotlight: 1-13 over hurdles; didn't go unbacked on chase debut when beaten under 4l by Loki's Mischief over 2m5f here recently and he's 7lb better off with the winner; clear claims.
runners.comment: Under 4l behind Loki's Mischief over 2m5f here recently and 7lb better off with the winner

runners.name: Axel Bleue
runners.spotlight: Point and dual bumper winner in 2025; runner-up in two 2m7f novice hurdles in October but pulled up on handicap debut the next month and again ran moderately on his return, albeit back in a novice.
runners.comment: Could appreciate returning to a handicap but current wellbeing is a bit of an issue

runners.name: Hell Hound
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdle winner who won on only his second run over fences at Southwell (3m, good), cosily by 3l; good chance off 6lb higher but might not appreciate too much rain (good to soft would be acceptable).
runners.comment: Southwell winner; fair shout off 6lb higher but might not appreciate too much rain
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate- Hell Hound

races.race_name: Adept GRP Cabinets Mares' Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Bridget Mary
runners.spotlight: Went very close at Chepstow (2m7f) before gifted the race over C&D last month when left clear late on; she did hold every chance at the time anyway and has gone up only 2lb.
runners.comment: Left clear here a month ago but she did hold every chance at the time; raised only 2lb

runners.name: Shesupincourt
runners.spotlight: Losing run is mounting up, as are a string of big defeats; has won over hurdles and fences but she needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Losing run is mounting up, as are a string of big defeats

runners.name: Lynsey Larue
runners.spotlight: Dual point winner; it was only her fourth run over fences when second at Warwick (3m, soft) and she could rebound from her Ludlow run now fitted with a tongue-tie; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Second at Warwick before a lesser show at Ludlow; tongue-tie could make a difference

runners.name: Just A Memory
runners.spotlight: Narrowly won small-field 2m5f handicap chase for Tom Ellis last September but her next two runs (3m2f, hurdle/chase) were very disappointing, as was her stable debut in January (hurdling); no headgear this afternoon; a bit risky.
runners.comment: Has won over fences but no positives to take from her last three races

runners.name: O'Grady's Hill
runners.spotlight: Beaten between 15l and 58l in seven starts for this yard, six of those handicap chases; the blinkers did nothing last time.
runners.comment: Beaten between 15l and 58l in seven starts for this yard, six of those handicap chases

runners.name: Miss Pearl
runners.spotlight: Went close at Bangor early last year and was placed again next time; however, her form ran cold afterwards and she finished well behind over hurdles last week after a layoff.
runners.comment: She finished well behind over hurdles last week after a layoff

races.race_name: Adept GRP Cabinets Mares' Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Forest Spirit
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner; beaten 5.5l over C&D (good) a month ago on first run for 187 days and could be sharper for that outing, and benefit from the new cheekpieces; one to consider.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner; beaten 5.5l over C&D (good) a month ago on first run for 187 days

runners.name: Jlow
runners.spotlight: 20-race maiden under both codes and did plenty wrong when pulled up last time at Warwick (2m5f); opposable.
runners.comment: 20-race maiden under both codes and did plenty wrong when pulled up last time

runners.name: La Belle Argentee
runners.spotlight: Few positives to take from her first three handicaps (2m-2m4f), the latest on first run since leaving Dan Skelton; had a wind operation.
runners.comment: Few positives to take from her first three handicaps (2m-2m4f); had a wind operation

runners.name: Kates Choice
runners.spotlight: It was her first placing when runner-up at Exeter (2m, good) but she was some 25l off the winner; fell early here two weeks ago; has enough to prove on balance, though she does probably stay this far.
runners.comment: 25l off the winner when second two runs back; fell early here two weeks ago

runners.name: Freddiemissmolli
runners.spotlight: Bumper efforts were fairly promising but her hurdles runs less so; new trainer fits a hood for handicap debut on what's her first run since December; others safer.
runners.comment: New trainer fits a hood for handicap debut on what's her first run since December

runners.name: Mya Du Clos
runners.spotlight: Quiet at about 2m over hurdles before going up to 3m on handicap debut at Southwell, a trip she might not have stayed; she's still got plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Perhaps failed to stay 3m on her handicap debut but she still has plenty to prove

runners.name: Barafundle Bay
runners.spotlight: Low-grade Flat winner in 2024; beaten 22l and 39l in her two runs over hurdles and makes stable/handicap debut after a lengthy break; may need the ground good or quicker.
runners.comment: Low-grade Flat winner in 2024; beaten 22l and 39l in her two runs over hurdles

races.race_name: Golf At Gowran Park Optional Claiming Race

runners.name: Independent Expert
runners.spotlight: Eight-time winner, most recently Naas handicap over this trip in March (soft); safely held both starts since but closely matched with Gloriously Glam on C&D run on these better terms.
runners.comment: Naas winner over this trip in March; favourite's chance on ratings

runners.name: Gloriously Glam
runners.spotlight: Second runner for the yard and stays better than Clonmacash, two wins last year coming at 1m, including claimer here; best of three runs this term came over C&D last month when fourth in decent fillies handicap; trip/ground to suit, definite contender.
runners.comment: Goes well here, claiming winner last year; trip/ground to suit, definite contender
runners.owner: Dooley Thoroughbreds & J Fairhurst & Deepak Dhand

runners.name: Salacious
runners.spotlight: Maiden went closest when beaten a nose in Dundalk handicap over this trip in February; good run at the Curragh subsequently (heavy) but stiff task from out of handicap at Naas last time; could go well, yard had a winner at Navan on Saturday.
runners.comment: Stiff task latest; down in grade and could go well; yard had winner at Navan on Saturday

runners.name: Green Kite
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden's best run came when runner-up in 1m Killarney maiden on testing ground last October; didn't feature on Limerick comeback run last month and looks to have a bit to do on ratings.
runners.comment: Didn't feature on Limerick comeback run and looks to have a bit to do on ratings

runners.name: West Sedona
runners.spotlight: Arizona gelding cost 6,000euros as yearling and later resold for 5,000euros; second foal; dam won in Spain, half-sister to winners Unexpected Party (smart 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase) and Dolphin Village (prolific 1m2f-1m5f); newcomer likely best watched.
runners.comment: Arizona gelding cost 6,000euros as yearling; newcomer likely best watched

runners.name: Profit Surge
runners.spotlight: Maiden was placed three times in AW handicaps in December/January, most recently respectable third in low-grade Roscommon handicap; plenty on at the weights here.
runners.comment: Low-grade maiden seemingly has plenty on at the weights

runners.name: Royal Sapphire
runners.spotlight: AW form over the winter didn't amount to much and last of 10 (never recovering from slow start) in Ballinrobe claimer earlier this month on turf debut; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Nothing so far, badly away in Ballinrobe claimer latest

races.race_name: Gowran Park Median Auction Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Sanctijude
runners.spotlight: Now 0-9 but has run plenty of solid races in defeat in maidens and most recently in Curragh fillies handicap when good fourth (7f, good); quite exposed but place claims at least back in maiden company.
runners.comment: Maiden with plenty of good runs; place claims at least back in maiden company

runners.name: Fleur De Provence
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to four winners inc Eternal Silence (Group-placed 1m; RPR 105) & Chiara Luna (6f/8.5f inc 2yo/US; 95); dam 1m2f-1m4f winner (inc Group 2; 117); easy to back 16-1, very promising Naas debut in late March when staying on to finish second (Sanctijude fourth) to a hot favourite over today's trip (soft), despite running green; will appreciate 1m+ in time but big player on that run.
runners.comment: Debut Naas second makes her a leading player

runners.name: Wildlife
runners.spotlight: Kameko filly cost 48,000gns as a yearling; closely related to 1m AW winner Mohatu (RPR 86), half-sister to 7f AW 2yo winner Sea Baaeed (95); dam 7f AW winner (99), closely related to Italian Derby winner Tuscan Gaze; respected source so debutante worth a market check.
runners.comment: Respected source so debutante worth a market check

runners.name: Bofa Beach
runners.spotlight: Kameko filly cost 25,000gns as a foal and 80,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to 4 winners including Le Tabou (French 6f-7f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 109) & Shockwaves (1m AW; 92); dam 1m winner (100); plenty of debut promise when fourth in good-class Curragh maiden last summer (good) but three months later never sighted in Goffs Million over same C&D on softer ground; has undoubted ability, major player on that debut run.
runners.comment: Major player on Curragh debut run last summer, may not want too much rain

runners.name: Magical Mollie
runners.spotlight: Ribchester filly is first foal of a 7.3f winner (RPR 94), half-sister to winners Electric Waves (5f 2yo Group 3), Appealing (useful 6f-1m) and Tut (7f 2yo); newcomer likely all the better for the experience.
runners.comment: Newcomer likely all the better for the experience

runners.name: Nellcote
runners.spotlight: Sottsass fillly is first foal of a 1m AW Listed winner (RPR 99), a half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Crossfirehurricane, out of US 1m stakes winning half-sister to BC Juvenile Fillies Turf and French 1,000 Guineas winner Flotilla; yard in top form and any market strength has to be noted on debut.
runners.comment: Yard in top form and any market strength has to be noted on debut

runners.name: Jolie Cailin
runners.spotlight: Coulsty filly a 44,000euros yearling; half-sister to 3 winners including Tomahawk King (7f AW 2yo; RPR 76) and Berry Edge (1m1f/1m2f Flat inc AW); dam 1m winner (78), out of Listed winning half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 2 winner Volochine; yard going well and worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: Coulsty filly a 44,000euros yearling; yard going well and worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Beo Go Deo
runners.spotlight: Due Diligence filly cost 5,000euros as a foal; first foal; dam maiden sister to winners Clouds Rest (5f 2yo) and Murqaab (6f), half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Streamline; always behind on Navan debut in October; tongue-tied on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Always behind on Navan debut in October; tongue-tied on seasonal return

runners.name: Themis
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Teutates (Group-placed 1m/1m2f inc AW; RPR 112) and Warmonger (multiple 2m inc AW; 95); dam 1m4f AW winner (77); 12-1 for debut at the Curragh (7f, good) last August and failed to beat a rival home; holds some fancy entries so clearly thought capable of better but McMonagle prefers Nellcote.
runners.comment: Poor debut run last August; thought capable of better but McMonagle prefers Nellcote

runners.name: Big Cypress
runners.spotlight: Dubawi half-brother to winners Yosemite Valley (6f/7f inc Listed; RPR 117) and Piz Badile (1m-10.6f inc Listed; 116); dam 1m1f-10.5f winner (inc Listed; 105); nicely improved on seasonal return when staying on from some way back into fourth (hung right) in 7f Limerick maiden last month; sure to appreciate this longer trip and could well have more to offer (McDonogh retains ride).
runners.comment: Limerick comeback run encouraging; likely more to offer at this longer trip

runners.name: Chamonix
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett gelding shaped okay on Curragh debut last month, better form three weeks later at Cork when finishing fourth in good-class maiden, having made much of running; McMonagle's choice over Big Cypress.
runners.comment: Cork run a step in the right direction; McMonagle's choice over Big Cypress

runners.name: Rolltight
runners.spotlight: Lucky Vega gelding soundly beaten on sole start for Jessica Harrington last year but much improved on yard debut over C&D early this month, briefly outpaced off home turn but rallying well for second; big player.
runners.comment: Recent C&D second augurs well; big player

runners.name: Ravens Can Fly
runners.spotlight: Raven's Pass gelding is first foal of a 1m winner (RPR 76); likely all the better for this debut run.
runners.comment: Raven's Pass likely all the better for this debut run

runners.name: Roman Sands
runners.spotlight: Romanized gelding; third foal; dam runner-up 7f/1m AW (RPR 70), half-sister to 7f Listed winner Telwaar; debutant likely best watched.
runners.comment: Debutant likely best watched

runners.name: Time Whisperer
runners.spotlight: Time Test gelding cost 30,000euros as a foal; half-brother to winners Razzam (5f; RPR 82) and Zeussina (Italian 8.5f/1m1f); dam 1m winner (90); unlikely source of a first-time out maiden winner but worth a market watch.
runners.comment: Unlikely source of a first-time out maiden winner but worth a market watch

runners.name: Little Wing
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly a 50,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Codiak (1m4f/1m6f inc AW; RPR 96), Raven's Cry (7f/1m inc 2yo/US; 96) and Our Queen (Australian 5.5f-7f); dam French Listed-placed 1m4f winner (99); bred to want further but debutante worth a market check.
runners.comment: Bred to want further but debutante worth a market check

runners.name: Uncle Goalie
runners.spotlight: Galileo Gold colt cost 30,000euros as a yearling; half-brother to Czech 6f-7f winner Arkhangelsk (inc 2yo); dam 1m2f AW winner (RPR 77), sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner Forgotten Voice; newcomer best watched unless market speaks.
runners.comment: Galileo Gold colt cost 30,000euros as a yearling; best watched unless market speaks

runners.name: Ryans Hope
runners.spotlight: Placed on belated debut early last year in Newcastle AW maiden (blinkered), reappearing early this month when never sighted in Ballinrobe maiden; more likely one for handicaps.
runners.comment: Reappeared this month when never sighted at Ballinrobe; more likely one for handicaps

runners.name: Flagstone Lady
runners.spotlight: US Navy Flag filly; E1,000 foal bought back for E21,000 as a yearling; half-sister to winners Wonder Kid (1m2f/1m4f, RPR 101), Bavardages (7f-1m2f inc 2yo/AW; 83) and Doves Of Peace (6f AW; 61); dam French Listed-placed 7f-1m1f winner (inc 2yo; 94); needs to improve plenty on last month's Cork debut effort.
runners.comment: Needs to improve plenty on last month's Cork debut effort

runners.name: Loughrea
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam poor maiden half-sister to winners Ready To Roc (5f 2yo/US 6f), Quirky Gertie (7f) and I Am Dandy (7f); plenty green, didn't run badly on debut at Leopardstown last October, not quite getting home over 1m1f on heavy ground; potential improver back in trip on better ground.
runners.comment: Debut promise at Leopardstown in October; potential improver back in trip on better ground

runners.name: Hassaniya
runners.spotlight: Sands Of Mali colt placed in a barrier trial last year before fair debut run in Naas maiden; comeback run at the Curragh in March was of a similar level; lot more likely needed (better ground may suit) and eligible for a mark after this.
runners.comment: Couple of fair efforts, looks more one for handicaps

runners.name: Kingcharles
runners.spotlight: Golden Horde colt a 38,000euros yearling; half-brother to four winners; dam French maiden; always behind on Navan debut last month, easily passed over.
runners.comment: Always behind on Navan debut last month, easily passed over

runners.name: Zammawar
runners.spotlight: Poor form over various trips, easily overlooked.
runners.comment: Poor form over various trips, easily overlooked

runners.name: Hello Garda
runners.spotlight: 6,500euros breeze-up purchase; closely related to juvenile winner Urban War; half-brother to two others winners; dam an unraced daughter of Sea The Stars; last of 8 on Naas debut last October; gelded since, can only be watched on return.
runners.comment: Last of 8 on Naas debut last October; gelded since, can only be watched on return

races.race_name: Membership At Gowran Park Golf Club Handicap

runners.name: Alto Sax
runners.spotlight: Two wins in claimers on turf in May 2024; out of form at Dundalk until winning an apprentice handicap there at the beginning of last month; broke slowly at Leopardstown but finished to good effect, beaten under 3l in a 23-runner field; definite chance.
runners.comment: Availed of a low mark at Dundalk early last month, finished well at Leopardstown last time

runners.name: Staywitherjohnny
runners.spotlight: Ran her best race so far when third behind facile winner Majestic King over C&D early this month; 25-1 then, deserves to be a prime fancy now, with the champion jockey maintaining the partnership.
runners.comment: Ran her best race so far when third at 25-1 over C&D, deserves to be a prime fancy now

runners.name: Hale Bopp
runners.spotlight: Has done a lot of racing since her last win which was in September 2023; fair sixth here on penultimate run, but will need to reverse to reverse form with Staywitherjohnny and Half Nutz; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, has done a lot of racing since her last win, sixth here on penultimate run

runners.name: Son Of Wind
runners.spotlight: Achieved a peak rating of 70 in 2024 based on consistent form over sprint trips at Chelmsford; regressive last season;  has failed to beat a single rival in two Dundalk races; tackles this trio for the first time with a lot to prove.
runners.comment: AW winner in Britain, has failed to beat a single rival in two Dundalk races, new trip now

runners.name: Ballymagreehan
runners.spotlight: Best form on AW at Dundalk, a winner and twice placed over this trip; appears well held by Staywitherjohnny on recent C&D running
runners.comment: Best form on AW at Dundalk, appears well held by Staywitherjohnny on recent C&D running

runners.name: Master Garvey
runners.spotlight: Modest strike rate (2-49), fair AW form during the winter; finished in mid-field over C&D last month and needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Modest strike rate (2-49), fair AW form during the winter, mid-field over C&D last month
runners.owner: Patrick M O'Brien/Larry Murray/Richard W Walsh/Derek Cummins

runners.name: Mythical Rock
runners.spotlight: Now with his fifth different trainer; gained an overdue breakthrough at Dundalk on his 26th start; perhaps unlucky not to finish closer at Limerick last month on stable debut, since he almost lost his footing on the final bend; placed 11 times, capable of reaching the money in this grade.
runners.comment: Perhaps unlucky not to finish closer on stable debut, almost lost his footing on last turn

runners.name: Rampage
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner, last turf success was in 2023; two AW wins since then; unplaced favourite at Dundalk on latest; has a bit to find with Alto Sax on previous Leopardstown running.
runners.comment: Last turf win was in 2023, two AW wins since then, unplaced favourite at Dundalk on latest
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney

runners.name: Hastily
runners.spotlight: Winner of four of her 42 races; most recent success was over 6f at Cork last July; not the most consistent sort and has rarely shown her best form at this trip.
runners.comment: Most recent win was over 6f at Cork last July, has rarely shown her best form at this trip

runners.name: Al Rojo Vivo
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens; last of ten over 6f at Sligo last year on her only handicap attempt.
runners.comment: Weak form in maidens, last of ten over 6f at Sligo last year on her only handicap attempt

runners.name: Half Nutz
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner whose slow-starting tendency is a worry; not a bad run when staying on for fifth over C&D on latest; might struggled to turn the tables on third-placed Staywitherjohnny.
runners.comment: Six-time winner whose slow-starting tendency is a worry, not a bad run over C&D on latest

runners.name: Oreeda
runners.spotlight: Has made no impression in three handicap attempts; on latest, met trouble in running at Tipperary (clipped heels and rider briefly lost an iron), so that run can probably be upgrade.
runners.comment: Has made no impression in three handicap attempts, met trouble in running at Tipperary

runners.name: Chipsrdown
runners.spotlight: Signs of progress when fifth over 7f on handicap debut at Leopardstown; failed to build on that over this trip at Killarney; visored for the first time; stable having a good year.
runners.comment: Fair fifth at Leopardstown in her first handicap, failed to build on that at Killarney

runners.name: River Derwent
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in four of his last six starts but now on a two-year losing sequence; beaten around 8l in fourth at Roscommon; allowance should be made for interference suffered under two furlongs down.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in four of his last six starts but now on a two-year losing sequence

runners.name: Just Another Eagle
runners.spotlight: Sole victory from 21 starts came in a 1m4f handicap Dundalk in December 2024; slipped up at Dundalk on her final 2025 start after an excellent second in a 23-runner handicap at Navan; however, that was 1m6f, and this trip may prove inadequate.
runners.comment: Slipped up at Dundalk on final 2025 start, best form at 1m4f/1m6f, this may be inadequate

runners.name: Bright Image
runners.spotlight: Produced effort at the Curragh on the first day of the 2024 season; absent since then, best watched in the circumstances.
runners.comment: Good effort at the Curragh on the first day of the 2024 season, absent since, best watched

runners.name: Lady Mairen
runners.spotlight: Very useful form at two, placed twice and fourth of eight in 1m Curragh Group 3; missed 3yo campaign; probably needed the run when an unplaced 3-1 chance on her return at Bellewstown and made amends in a Sligo handicap; this is a furlong shorter, but she quickened well to assert; should have no problem with the trip and this better ground.
runners.comment: Very useful form at two, missed last season, got career back on track with a handicap win

runners.name: Sparan Nua
runners.spotlight: Made a good first impression when winning a 1m maiden at Naas 11 days ago at the main expense of the once-raced favourite;  sent off at 25-1, although trainer remarked that she has been showing plenty at home; appeals as a filly who could progress to Pattern level with experience.
runners.comment: Made a good first impression when winning a 1m maiden at Naas, potentially smart filly

runners.name: Vauntingly
runners.spotlight: Favourite for a maiden over C&D on debut early this month; had plenty to do after breaking slowly and stayed on well in the closing stages to finish 4 3/4l fourth behind a stablemate of Lady Mairen; gets 7lb from the three-year-old winner, and could go close.
runners.comment: Took a while to get the hang of things when third over C&D on debut, improvement likely

runners.name: Marmeladova
runners.spotlight: Went close in a 1m maiden at Navan on the third of five starts last season; ended the campaign with less encouraging runs at Dundalk and Naas; a revelation on reappearance at Ballinrobe, winning very easily from an 85-rated favourite; a one-pound rise to 76 appears surprisingly lenient; if accurate, she will need to take a big forward step to hold her own in this company.
runners.comment: Hacked up in a maiden at Ballinrobe, further progress needed to cope with younger rivals

runners.name: Whats New
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in two runs in maiden hurdles (soundly beaten both times) since a below-par Flat run at Navan; last season's Roscommon win was gained over 1m4f; later ran well over 1m6f at the Curragh, so no major stamina doubt.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in two runs over hurdles since a below-par Flat run at Navan

runners.name: Master Sydney
runners.spotlight: Won a mares' bumper at Roscommon last July (good); fair effort on Flat handicap debut at Down Royal (1m5f, yielding); 1m2f too sharp on seasonal return; recent Roscommon sixth was satisfactory; champion jockey booked, new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Good-ground bumper winner, recent Roscommon sixth was satisfactory, champion jockey booked

runners.name: Medieval Night
runners.spotlight: Pick of six runs for Dermot Weld when runner-up in 1m1f Leopardstown handicap (good) last August; steps up in trip after two moderate runs for this stable; plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Placed over 1m1f for Dermot Weld, steps up in trip after two moderate runs for this stable

runners.name: Good Onya Mate
runners.spotlight: Always at the back when 25-1 at Ballinrobe in his first handicap; his chance is not obvious, but the stable had a massive-priced double at Naas on Sunday.
runners.comment: Chance is not obvious, but the stable had a massive-priced double at Naas on Sunday
runners.owner: P J Morgan & Mrs S L Morgan

runners.name: Eternal Echo
runners.spotlight: Bumper/maiden hurdle winner for Gordon Elliott; has been running over shorter trips in maidens and could be a dark horse on handicap debut; not disgraced at this venue on the first of his two Flat starts for this stable.
runners.comment: Bumper/maiden hurdle winner for Gordon Elliott, could be a dark horse on handicap debut

runners.name: Celtic Druid
runners.spotlight: Progressive in winning back-to-back 1m4f handicaps at Dundalk in March/April; had to wait for a gap to open at Navan last time  and ran on well to take fourth, 1 3/4l in front of Earthsong; very capable 7lb claimer is starting to establish herself; strong contender.
runners.comment: Maintained AW progression with Navan fourth, 7lb claimer is starting to establish herself

runners.name: Quiz Test
runners.spotlight: Sole win came at Cork in 2023; decent form last autumn, including a close third over 1m4f at Galway and second in a 19-runner contest at the Curragh, with Whats New, Earthsong and Bruant behind; may improve from a Cork run, but has to reverse form with Celtic Drud and Earthsong.
runners.comment: Almost three years since his only win, fair form last autumn, may improve from a Cork run

runners.name: Eastern Blue Bird
runners.spotlight: Modest form in bumpers and over hurdles; 50-1 when in rear at Dundalk on his Flat handicap debut; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Modest form in bumpers and over hurdles, in rear at Dundalk on his Flat handicap debut

runners.name: Earthsong
runners.spotlight: Best effort came on handicap debut over 1m6f at Navan on yielding ground last August; produced another solid display at that venue last month, prominent all the way before losing fourth place to Celtic Druid; that rival finished well after getting into the clear and may uphold the form.
runners.comment: Has shown winning potential in staying handicaps, might not cope with Celtic Druid here

runners.name: Bruant
runners.spotlight: Two Flat wins in France; no sign of his best form since a novice hurdle win at Downpatrick last summer; premature to write off now that he has dropped in the ratings, but appears well held by Celtic Druid on Navan running; first-time with hood
runners.comment: Two Flat wins in France, no sign of his best form since a novice hurdle win last summer

runners.name: Sonic Invader
runners.spotlight: Ran in three maidens early last season; was entitled to need the run at Navan after a year-long absence; should be sharper now; Colin Keane takes over from an apprentice.
runners.comment: Was entitled to need the run at Navan after a year-long absence, should be sharper now

runners.name: Miss Gitana
runners.spotlight: Three Flat wins, twice in Britain and over 2m at Tramore last April for Gordon Elliott; disappointing over hurdles on last two starts in 2025; down the field on stable debut in the Navan race in which Celtic Druid was fourth.
runners.comment: Down the field on stable debut in the Navan race in which Celtic Druid was fourth

runners.name: Paradise Lost
runners.spotlight: Placed over hurdles on his two most recent outings; two undistinguished Flat runs last season; better judged on the form he showed near the end of the 2024 campaign, although seems best suited by soft ground.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles on his two most recent starts, two undistinguished Flat runs last year

runners.name: Mr Rango
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m2f on soft or heavy, latest over two years ago; fair form this season but needs to find extra; ran in the 7.50 at Roscommon on Monday.
runners.comment: Fair form this season but needs to find extra, ran in the 7.50 at Roscommon on Monday

runners.name: Jurality
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 23 starts (six over hurdles), didn't really warrant his strong favouritism at Leopardstown last week although ran well when third to an all-the-way winner over 1m5f (good); up further in trip here.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden but last week's Leopardstown run gives him claims if staying

runners.name: Mephisto
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick in handicap hurdles last summer before losing his way; last month's AW return was encouraging though (neck off Desert Friend) and of definite interest here off 8lb lower turf mark with Keane booked.
runners.comment: Promising recent AW return; definite interest here off 8lb lower turf mark

runners.name: God The Highness
runners.spotlight: Maiden went closest when touched off over 1m6f at Sligo last summer off 60; didn't count on last month's Navan comeback run, drops in grade now.
runners.comment: Maiden didn't count on last month's Navan comeback run, drops in grade now

runners.name: Desert Friend
runners.spotlight: Veteran has done a bit of everything and recorded his fifth Flat win early this month when landing 1m6f Sligo apprentice handicap in emphatic fashion under today's rider (yielding-to-soft); 8lb rise allows him to remain in this grade and has won over 2m.
runners.comment: Recent Sligo winner under this rider; 8lb rise allows him to remain in this grade

runners.name: Timandi
runners.spotlight: Yet to trouble the judge in 12 attempts over various trips, returning from AW campaign when midfield in Curragh 1m handicap in March; others preferred.
runners.comment: Yet to trouble the judge in 12 attempts over various trips; others preferred
runners.owner: Mrs N K Griffin & T Brosnan

runners.name: Synchronize
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden well held in handicap hurdles of late; down to basement mark on the Flat but remains opposable.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose maiden down to basement mark on the Flat but remains opposable

runners.name: Velvet And Vine
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been absent since early last year; career-low mark but hard to be confident in.
runners.comment: Maiden absent since early last year; career-low mark but hard to be confident in

runners.name: Appian Way
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner is now 0-17 on the Flat and hard to see him breaking that sequence here.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner is now 0-17 on the Flat and hard to see him breaking that sequence

runners.name: Our Lucky Lady
runners.spotlight: Sligo maiden hurdle winner last October, safely held on handicap debut at recent Punchestown festival; only glimpses of form on the Flat in recent years and hard to fancy; hood tried.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner but little of late on Flat so hard to fancy; hood tried

runners.name: Cala Gracioneta
runners.spotlight: Poor form form in AW handicaps over the winter; hooded now for turf return and remains opposable off this mark.
runners.comment: Poor form form in AW handicaps over the winter; hooded now for turf return

runners.name: Almenfee
runners.spotlight: German winner (1m4f, good) has failed to fire in five runs for this yard on softer ground, most recently over 1m4f at Limerick last month; up in trip now but forcecast rain a negative.
runners.comment: German winner yet to fire for this yard; wants good ground

runners.name: Among The Pines
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden wasn't been sighted in three maidens ad again on handicap debut (upset in stalls) last October for Michael Grassick; bought for 1,000euros in November and debuts for new yard over a much longer trip.
runners.comment: Little of note last year; bought for small money and remains badly handicapped

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Havana Sprite
