runners.spotlight: Lacked the professionalism, as much as the pace, of the three who beat her at Newmarket 17 days ago (5f, good to firm) but it was an encouraging debut with her greenness in mind; open to plenty of improvement; strong claims if coping with the slower ground.
runners.comment: Hugely promising debut at Newmarket 17 days ago; leading claims with improvement likely

runners.name: Halliwell Stream
runners.spotlight: January foal; 140,000gns yearling; first foal of a Group-placed 5f 2yo winner (RPR 92), herself closely related to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Queen Of Bermuda; stable, who also run Harlequin Sky, having first 2yo runners of the year; a newcomer of note.
runners.comment: 140,000gns yearling; first foal of a Group-placed 5f 2yo winnner; a newcomer of note

runners.name: Havana Lightning
runners.spotlight: Only fifth of eight on her Newmarket debut last month (5f, good) but she was notably strong in the market that day (6-5 favourite) and the race looks a solid piece of form; surprising if she couldn't leave that well behind her in time.
runners.comment: Failed to justify strong favouritism on Newmarket debut; worth another chance

runners.name: Havana Grey Star
runners.spotlight: 100-30 third favourite for her Nottingham debut 11 days ago (6f, good), a race where she travelled strongly but then looked an extremely difficult ride under pressure; possible that was just greenness and she can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Looked a hard ride when coming off the bridle on Nottingham debut; can do better

runners.name: Sydney Grace
runners.spotlight: April foal; 40,000euros yearling; second foal; half-sister to 7f winner Teraabb (RPR 87); dam French/US 7f-8.5f winner (including 2yo/Listed; 100); stable have had a couple of winning 2yo newcomers this year; check the betting.
runners.comment: 40,000euros half-sister to a fair 7f winner; yard's 2yos going well; check market

runners.name: Persian Sunset
runners.spotlight: Useful pedigree but ran to just a modest level when a 7l third of five at Beverley 16 days ago; open to improvement but it is a requirement.
runners.comment: Modest form when 3rd of five on debut at Beverley 16 days ago; can do better but needs to

runners.name: Harlequin Sky
runners.spotlight: February foal; 20,000gns breeze-up 2yo; ninth foal; half-sister to six winners including In The Breeze (1m2f/1m4f; RPR 91), He's A Rock Star (French 7f-1m5f including 2yo) and Allfredandnobell (2m-2m6f hurdle); dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f Group 1/Grade 1 winner Storming Home; one of two newcomers for Jane Chapple-Hyam.
runners.comment: 20,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-sister to six winners; one of two newcomers for the yard

runners.name: Sea Of Sardinia
runners.spotlight: 33-1 when last of eight on her Newmarket debut (behind Havana Lightning) five weeks ago; now tongue tied; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: 33-1 when last of eight at Newmarket last month; now tried in a tongue tie

runners.name: Minnie Bear
runners.spotlight: Dropped right away to finish last of the eight runners on her Wolverhampton debut six weeks ago (5f; 22-1 into 14-1); needs to leave that well behind and probably a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Well-beaten last of eight on last month's Wolverhampton debut; longer-term prospect

runners.name: Alpaca's Bullet
runners.spotlight: 40-1 for her recent Leicester debut (5f, good to firm), a race where she was hampered at the start before failing to threaten; needs to leave that well behind.
runners.comment: Never dangerous when 8th of ten at Leicester 11 days ago (40-1); big step forward needed

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bassrah
runners.spotlight: Sent off the 9-2 third favourite for her debut at Nottingham last month (6f, good) but she ran green and never featured; should do better in time but there are some interesting newcomers for her to deal with today.
runners.comment: Didn't live up to market billing on debut but still has time to do much better

runners.name: Conciliate
runners.spotlight: 22,000gns foal, 34,000gns yearling; second foal of a 5f-6f winner (including Listed; RPR 104); stable yet to strike with 2yos (as of Monday) in 2026; betting to guide.
runners.comment: 34,000gns yearling; dam a Listed sprint winner (RPR 104); betting to guide

runners.name: Peaceful Warrior
runners.spotlight: Third foal; brother to winners Brindi (US/Canadian 5f-7f including 2yo/Listed) and Boston Rose (French 7.5f turf 2yo/US 1m AW); dam 6f AW 2yo winner (RPR 83), sister to 6f Group 1 winner Emaraaty Ana; Kevin Ryan's juveniles have made a fast start to the season and he looks a strong contender.
runners.comment: Brother to two winners out of well-related 2yo winner; stable's juveniles going well

runners.name: Boysofwallstreet
runners.spotlight: 85,000gns foal, £95,000 yearling; fifth foal; brother to Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Pearl Glory (RPR 98), closely related to 6f/7f winner Guiteau (78); dam once-raced half-sister to Group-placed 6f/7f winner Louis The Pious; looks the part on paper and the betting should be revealing.
runners.comment: £95,000 yearling; two winning siblings, one with an RPR of 98; likely type on paper

runners.name: Rogue Imperial
runners.spotlight: 77,000gns yearling, 170,000gns breeze-up 2yo; second foal; half-brother to 7f 2yo winner Goodwood Galaxy (RPR 100); dam 7f/1m winner (77), half-sister to 7f 2yo (Moyglare Stud) and 1m (Irish 1,000 Guineas) Group 1 winner Fallen Angel; lots to like on paper and Archie Watson's 2yos have hit the ground running.
runners.comment: 170,000gns breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to useful 2yo winner Goodwood Galaxy; likely type

runners.name: Ardad Steve
runners.spotlight: 32,000gns yearling; first foal of a once-raced half-sister to winners Hadaatha (1m2f Listed) and Itlaaq (prolific 1m2f-2m), out of 1m Listed winner/1,000 Guineas third; stable have had a winning 2yo newcomer this year; betting to guide.
runners.comment: 32,000gns yearling; first foal of a well-related maiden; betting useful on debut

runners.name: Easy Answer
runners.spotlight: 70,000gns yearling; second foal of a maiden from the family of top stayer Gildoran; stable's 2yos usually better for a run and likely best watched unless strong in the market.
runners.comment: 70,000gns yearling; dam a maiden from family of top stayer Gildoran; likely best watched

runners.name: Prince Kameo
runners.spotlight: 18-1 and in need of the experience on his Lingfield debut three weeks ago (6f, AW); can do better but such progress will be needed.
runners.comment: 18-1 and green when 4th of seven on last month's Lingfield debut (6f, AW); can do better

runners.name: Jack Sparowe
runners.spotlight: On a losing run and can get it wrong at the start; well handicapped on 2025 best, however, and he stayed on for fourth at Newcastle 19 days ago; conditions shouldn't be an issue and he commands respect with new blinkers now replacing cheekpieces.
runners.comment: On a losing run but well treated and the change of headgear could give him a lift

runners.name: Law Supreme
runners.spotlight: His good run of form ground to a halt at Thirsk last time but he can be given a pass as he was tight for room at a crucial stage; Billy Loughnane back on board today and his record for him reads 4111; one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Had an excuse last time and his record for Billy Loughnane makes him of some interest

runners.name: Goldie Trickett
runners.spotlight: Some promise for William Haggas last year and again when third of ten on her stable/seasonal debut last month (1m, AW; hooded first time); this sister to her new yard's Group 1 1m winner Saffron Beach isn't sure to improve for the longer trip but she isn't fully exposed just yet.
runners.comment: Fair third on stable/seasonal debut last month; steps up to 1m2f with stamina not assured

runners.name: Dream Pirate
runners.spotlight: Two course wins on slower than good, the latest off 2lb lower last September; added a 1m2f success at Lingfield in January and his subsequent modest effort at Nottingham may have been needed after three-months off; more appealing than many.
runners.comment: Two course wins (1m) and he has won over this trip on AW; one to take seriously

runners.name: Solanna
runners.spotlight: Nine wins to his name, including C&D, and just 1lb higher than for March's narrow Kempton success (1m3f); two creditable efforts have followed but he could use the ground drying out if he is to add to his tally.
runners.comment: Multiple winner, including C&D, and he arrives in good form; good or faster preferable

runners.name: Special Breeze
runners.spotlight: She has not been beaten far in two handicaps at around 1m this spring, keeping on for a 2l fifth at Southwell three weeks ago; still has some potential and stepping up to 1m2f could unlock it.
runners.comment: Two encouraging handicap runs last month; step up to 1m2f could unlock further progress
runners.owner: Michael H Sly Dr T Davies Mrs Pam Sly

runners.name: Urban Warrior
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three maiden/novice runs but there was more promise to be gleaned from his handicap debut fourth at Wolverhampton (1m6f, AW; well supported) in January, a race where he still looked to be learning his craft; the hood worn on that occasion is now dispensed with; capable of better.
runners.comment: Improved when 4th on handicap debut in January (1m6f, AW); hood left off today; unexposed
runners.owner: W Enticknap, B Ralph And Partner

runners.name: Relocal
runners.spotlight: The winner of one of his 11 starts for Roger Varian, namely a 13.3f handicap at Chelmsford in January; sold for 8,500gns last month; still to prove as good on turf as AW but that is perhaps circumstance, rather than a marked preference for artificial surfaces.
runners.comment: Chance on best form for R Varian; more to prove on turf but not discounted on stable debut

runners.name: Betelgeuse
runners.spotlight: Ran away with a Class 6 handicap at Southwell last October (1m6f) off a 10lb lower mark; two runner-up finishes at Southwell last month (after a 166-day absence) and he seemed fully effective on turf last year; one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts at Southwell last month; seems fully effective on turf; in the mix again

runners.name: Saratoga Gold
runners.spotlight: Losing run stretches back to July 2023 but he ended 2025 in good form and his reappearance at Doncaster (1m4f, good to firm) 18 days ago was a positive one; stepping back up in distance is a positive but he could do with the ground drying out.
runners.comment: On losing run but mark is in range & shaped okay on his return; drying ground would help

runners.name: Dereham
runners.spotlight: Shed his maiden tag in the corresponding race in 2021 (good to soft) and he has added a further four wins, the last three in consecutive renewals of a 2m handicap at Newmarket; chased home a stablemate in this race last year after a six-month absence; back from eight months off today and perhaps vulnerable to younger legs.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2021 and 2nd in it last year; may come on for this after eight months out

runners.name: Golden Garden
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a 1m6f handicap at Wolverhampton for Jamie Osborne last September; unplaced in two hurdle runs for this yard but he didn't run badly returned to the Flat at Goodwood 19 days ago (1m6f, good); down in class today and should have a big run in him.
runners.comment: 1m6f AW win for Jamie Osborne; solid 4th back on Flat 19 days ago; contender down in class

runners.name: Iwantmytimewithyou
runners.spotlight: Won two handicaps last July (1m4f, good and 1m6f, firm); he has finished second in two of his three runs this year, latterly beaten a short-head at Leicester 11 days ago (1m4f, good to firm); had just one run on slower than good and finished well beaten, so conditions may be a concern.
runners.comment: Knocking at the door this year and could yet rate higher; slower than good ground a query

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap

runners.name: Poke The Bear
runners.spotlight: Three wins at Lingfield (7f-1m) over the winter; his form has cooled more recently and he returns to turf (well held in one attempt) with a point to prove; can blow the start.
runners.comment: In good form on AW over the winter but below par on last three runs; more to prove on turf

runners.name: Timely Affair
runners.spotlight: Steady progress since handicapping and he took his form to a new level with his ready defeat of Ribston Pippin at Southwell three weeks ago (1m, AW; cheekpieces first time); up 6lb but open to further progress and he should go well if the ground is suitable (smart half-sister Royal Velvet happiest on good or faster).
runners.comment: Improved for cheekpieces with ready AW win latest; up 6lb but could have more to offer

runners.name: Tilani
runners.spotlight: Galway winner for Dermot Weld last summer; first win for new stable (third attempt) when comfortably seeing off 12 rivals at Newbury last week (1m, good) and he escapes a penalty for that apprentice-race success; of obvious interest dropped into a Class 6.
runners.comment: Unpenalised for his ready Class 5 win last week (1m, good); obvious chance of following up

runners.name: Cezarro
runners.spotlight: 0-12 but he has shown winning potential since joining Ross Burdon, including a second of nine at Kempton in March; each-way shout now back on turf with cheekpieces returning.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden but running well since joining current yard; each-way shout back on turf

runners.name: Ribston Pippin
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last summer (good to firm) and he added to his haul with a ready win at Southwell (1m, AW) in December; chased home Timely Affair back at Southwell three weeks ago (1m) but while he is 4lb better off today, he wouldn't be sure to reverse the placings (raced awkwardly under pressure).
runners.comment: C&D winner; second to Timely Affair latest; 4lb turnaround may be insufficient though

runners.name: Charming Fellow
runners.spotlight: Won a Pontefract nursery for Peter Chapple-Hyam in October 2024 (1m, soft); in good form last autumn too but he has been sent off at big prices and finished down the field in three runs for his new stable this year; could prove a different proposition back on turf, especially if the going remains on the slow side, and cheekpieces return today; check betting.
runners.comment: Quiet for new yard (AW) but may prove a different proposition back on turf; check betting

runners.name: Havana Tobouggaloo
runners.spotlight: Placed on a couple of occasions last season and she ran to a higher level on her seasonal return, going down narrowly in a 1m handicap at Bath last month (1m, good to soft); the winner there has followed up since and the pair were well clear of the third so a 3lb rise looks justified; solid contender.
runners.comment: Fine 2nd on seasonal return last month; up 3lb but still looks a leading contender

runners.name: Kamaway
runners.spotlight: Unable to justify favouritism on her handicap debut in March (7f, AW) but she showed her true colours with a comfortable C&D win (good to soft) last week; well in under her penalty and she has more to offer; solid claims.
runners.comment: Impressive over C&D last week (good to soft); well in under a penalty; major player

runners.name: Emery Down
runners.spotlight: Of interest on her best AW efforts for John Butler and her second run for this yard was better than her first; needs another step forward if she is to come out on top and still has a bit to prove on the grass.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but her best form has come on AW and others hold stronger claims

runners.name: Lastchancezouloon
runners.spotlight: Bits of form for Richard Fahey but she finished down the field in two runs for Jack Channon in late 2025; sold for 1,000gns in January; upped in trip and tried in a hood on her stable debut.
runners.comment: New trip/headgear offer hope of better on stable debut but others look far more persuasive

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: Noble Guest
runners.spotlight: Two C&D wins (good and good to soft) last year and he comes here just 2lb higher than for the latter success; promising reappearance last month but he failed to fire on soft ground here last week; type to bounce back.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins last year; promising return last month before a low-key run on soft last week

runners.name: Giorgio M
runners.spotlight: Made all to win a 7f AW handicap at Lingfield in December, his second start for Mark Loughnane; in and out since that success but no doubt he is handicapped to go well and he has run well over C&D in the past (including finishing second in this race year off 4lb higher); one to consider with Billy Loughnane back in the saddle.
runners.comment: On a good mark, 4lb lower than for his close 2nd in this race last year; one to consider

runners.name: Tennessee Gold
runners.spotlight: Three wins in 2024 but he has drawn a blank since; returns to his optimum trip off a reduced mark and this is his first Class 5 handicap for some time; not solid but he has the ability to go well.
runners.comment: Won three times in 2024 but drawn a blank since; drops in class and back to optimum trip

runners.name: Tactical Plan
runners.spotlight: Won twice on slow ground in the autumn of 2024; in and out in his three runs this year but his Leicester fourth last month (7f, good) brings him into the reckoning; a visor is now tried (ran well in one attempt in cheekpieces); further rainfall would aid his chance.
runners.comment: Mixed record in 2026 but on a handy mark if the new headgear works the oracle

runners.name: Berry Clever
runners.spotlight: His three wins have all come at Kempton (7f/1m) but he is fully effective on turf, runner-up over C&D (soft) last week; won't be far away with a repeat.
runners.comment: All wins on AW but fully effective on turf; solid second over C&D last week; considered

runners.name: Middleton View
runners.spotlight: He made a winning start for Stuart Williams when strong at the finish over 5f at Chelmsford in December; little to cheer since and hopes are pinned on the return to 7f/turf having a positive effect.
runners.comment: 5f AW win on stable debut in December; little to cheer since and now steps back up in trip

runners.name: Uppercase
runners.spotlight: Promise in maiden/novice company and again when second of nine on his handicap debut at Pontefract last summer (1m, good); below that level two weeks later and again on his return to action six weeks ago; now has a point to prove but still brings low mileage.
runners.comment: Promising start to career but his last two runs have seen his form dip; still low mileage

runners.name: Ziggy's Condor
runners.spotlight: The winner of three of his 34 starts for Richard Fahey, the latest off this mark at Newcastle in January (6f); sold for 19,000gns last month; capable at this level and the betting should be revealing starting out for Adam Kirby.
runners.comment: Has been rated a fair bit higher in the past; betting to guide starting out for Adam Kirby

runners.name: Rogue Allegiance
runners.spotlight: Didn't kick on from his winning debut for David O'Meara; down in the weights as a result and he has run two solid races for his new stable, second over 6f at Leicester last month before a good third over C&D four weeks ago (good to firm); untried on slower than good but handicapped to win.
runners.comment: Two promising runs for his new yard this spring; handicapped to win; slower than good a ?

runners.name: Chilly Breeze
runners.spotlight: Early-season 5f win as a 2yo when trained by David Evans; unplaced in two handicap runs last summer; still has low mileage and it will be interesting to see how he goes in the market prior to this stable debut (same owners).
runners.comment: 5f win as a 2yo; quiet last year; low mileage but likely best watched on stable debut

races.race_name: Unibet/British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C & D) (GBB)

runners.name: Frumoasa
runners.spotlight: 18-1 and made a winning start in 5f maiden at Southwell three weeks ago, getting up late on; not taken lightly under a 7lb penalty with the step up to 6f looking a big plus for this Supremacy filly.
runners.comment: Got up late to make winning start at Southwell; not taken lightly with 6f looking big plus

runners.name: Lady Branksome
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling, 60,000gns 2yo; Saxon Warrior filly; half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Sovereign Thought (RPR 74); dam, unplaced over 6f-9.4f (RPR 57), is a half-sister to useful 1m-1m4f winner Vivat Rex, out of 1m/1m1f Listed winning sister to Oaks winner Imagine; a considered debutante, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Saxon Warrior filly; a considered debutante, especially if the market vibes are positive

runners.name: It'sbeenemotional
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; half-sister to three winners, notably Il Bandito (7f/1m AW; RPR 96); dam Group-placed 7f/7.5f winner (RPR 99); this Space Blues filly can't be discounted.
runners.comment: A half-sister to three winners, so this Space Blues filly can't be discounted

runners.name: Heldobeldo
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns foal, 23,000gns yearling; Kameko filly; dam, unplaced over 6f-1m (RPR 46), is closely related to 6f 2yo winner Elizabeth Bennet and a half-sister to Italian 7.5f 2yo Listed winner Championofmyheart; stable continues among the winners so she's another newcomer to consider.
runners.comment: Stable continues among the winners so she's another newcomer to consider

runners.name: Madentine
runners.spotlight: 16,000gns yearling; first foal; dam, unplaced from 7.7f-1m4f (RPR 76), is closely related to multiple HK 1m2f/1m4f Group 1 winner Exultant; daughter of Invincible Army is in good hands and enters calculations.
runners.comment: This daughter of Invincible Army is in good hands and enters calculations

runners.name: So Tempting
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns yearling; Sergei Prokofiev filly; half-sister to 7f AW 2yo winner Doitforandrew (RPR 95); dam, unplaced at 1m (RPR 48), is a half-sister to 6f Group 3 winner Distinctive and useful 5f winner Blue Aegean; appeals on paper and she's not out of things on this introduction.
runners.comment: Sergei Prokofiev filly; appeals on paper and she's not out of things on this debut

runners.name: Bearly Blue
runners.spotlight: 90,000gns foal; Kodi Bear filly; dam, 5f AW winner (RPR 66), is a half-sister to winners Kind Of Blue (6f Group 1) and Blue Anthem (5f/7f), out of unraced sister to 6f Group 2 winner Deacon Blues and half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner The Tin Man; interesting newcomer for yard which has taken past two runnings of this.
runners.comment: Kodi Bear filly; interesting newcomer for yard which has taken past two runnings of this
runners.owner: G Kennington, J Henwood & E Kelly

runners.name: Santo
runners.spotlight: A'ali filly; half-sister to three winners, notably Whenthedealinsdone (5f/6f AW; RPR 102); dam, 6f/7f winner (Group 3; RPR 107), is out of half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Spinola; possibilities.
runners.comment: A'ali filly who is a half-sister to three winners; possibilities on her first run

runners.name: Fortunate
runners.spotlight: Failed to build on debut Wolverhampton sixth when last of 13 in 5f Bath novice last month; she needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Failed to build on debut Wolverhampton sixth when last of 13 in 5f Bath novice last month

runners.name: Magnesium
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and hinted at promise when a never-dangerous seventh of 12 in novice at Ascot (5f, good) on her debut 12 days ago; this Ubettabelieveit filly can take a step forward.
runners.comment: Never-dangerous seventh at Ascot on her debut 12 days ago; she can take a step forward

races.race_name: Unibet Support Safer Gambling Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C & D) (GBB) (Div I)

runners.name: Spirit Of Saxony
runners.spotlight: Saxon Warrior gelding who shaped well in a trio of novices (6f to 1m) before posting a good fifth of 11 on his handicap debut at Ascot (1m) 12 days ago, fading last 1f; the clear form pick.
runners.comment: Shaped well in novices before good fifth on his h'cap debut at Ascot; the clear form pick

runners.name: Deported
runners.spotlight: Territories gelding; in the frame on all his four runs to date, returning with a good fourth on his handicap debut at Southwell (1m) last month; this keen-going sort holds solid claims back down to 7f.
runners.comment: Good fourth on handicap debut at Southwell last month; holds solid claims back at 7f

runners.name: No Nay Data
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on her debut when a C&D second in October and she comfortably landed the odds on her return in 7f Southwell novice in February; this No Nay Never filly has more to offer and must enter calculations after another break.
runners.comment: Comfortably won at Southwell in February; she must enter calculations after a break

runners.name: Hanaan
runners.spotlight: 55,000gns yearling; Dandy Man filly; sister to 6f/7f winner Shared Belief (RPR 95); dam, 7f winner (RPR 82), is closely related to 6f Listed winner Cape Factor; has undergone breathing surgery ahead of her debut.
runners.comment: Dandy Man filly; has undergone breathing surgery ahead of her debut

runners.name: Manly Fireball
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden who wasn't disgraced on his first go in handicaps when seventh at Lingfield (6f, AW) 22 days ago; not out of things back in novice company on his first go over 7f.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on h'cap debut when seventh at Lingfield latest; not out of things up to 7f

runners.name: Chasse Patate
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; Kodi Bear filly; half-sister to 6f winner Bonus Time (RPR 93); dam, 6f AW winner (RPR 94), is a half-sister to 2m hurdle winner Kristjano; in good hands so this newcomer needs considering.
runners.comment: Kodi Bear filly; in good hands so this newcomer needs considering

runners.name: Screen Actor
runners.spotlight: $100,000 yearling, 40,000gns 2yo; dam, US Graded stakes-placed 1m/8.5f turf/dirt winner, is a half-sister to UAE 1m Group 2 winner Barbecue Eddie; the market can guide for this unraced son of Twirling Candy.
runners.comment: The market can guide for this unraced son of Twirling Candy

runners.name: Mythological Star
runners.spotlight: 19,000euros yearling; Magna Grecia gelding; closely related to Group-placed 1m-1m2f winner Astro King (RPR 116) and a half-brother to four other winners, notably Piz Gloria (French 1m; RPR 91); dam French 9.5f AW winner (RPR 83); the market can guide for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Closely related to Group-placed 1m-1m2f winner Astro King; market can guide for newcomer

runners.name: Santiago Boy
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and green when very slowly away before seventh of 11 in 7f maiden at Wolverhampton on his debut 23 days ago; this son of Mayson can take a fair step forward.
runners.comment: Very slowly away when seventh at Wolverhampton on his debut; can take a fair step forward

runners.name: Drymee
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and green but promising debut fourth of 11 in 7f noivce at Leiecester (7f, good to firm) in September for Joseph Parr; gelded/changed hands for 11,000 guineas subsequently and he's open to a fair bit of progress for his new yard.
runners.comment: Promising debut fourth at Leicester in September for Joseph Parr; open to progress

races.race_name: Unibet Support Safer Gambling Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C & D) (GBB) (Div II)

runners.name: Hamda's Joy
runners.spotlight: Fair form when placed on all her three runs to date, staying on into third of nine in 6f Wolverhampton novice last month; not out of things with this step up to 7f a likely plus.
runners.comment: Good third at Wolverhampton last month; not out of it with step up to 7f a likely plus

runners.name: The Golden Snitch
runners.spotlight: Galileo Gold gelding who failed to build on an encouraging 7f debut fourth at Doncaster when only 19th in 1m Newbury maiden last month; he's worth another chance.
runners.comment: Didn't build on promising debut 4th when only 19th at Newbury latest; worth another chance

runners.name: House Of Medici
runners.spotlight: Bated Breath colt; dam, 1m4f winner (RPR 91), is a half-sister to winners Marksman Queen (US 1m3f Grade 3), Signore Fox (Australian 6f Group 3) and Oh Susanna (South African 1m1f/1m2f Grade 1), out of US 1m1f Grade 2 winner; appeals on paper so this newcomer rates a player for his in-form yard.
runners.comment: Bated Breath colt; appeals on paper so this newcomer rates a player for his in-form yard

runners.name: Miss Gallant
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns yearling; Pinatubo filly; half-sister to winners Markakol (5f; RPR 90) and Maissara (6f AW; RPR 81); dam, 5f winner (Listed; RPR 104), is a half-sister to smart 5f/6f winner Tone The Barone; hooded for her debut and she's no forlorn hope.
runners.comment: Pinatubo filly; hooded for her debut and she's no forlorn hope

runners.name: Crossbar
runners.spotlight: 9,000euros foal, 37,000gns yearling; Phoenix Of Spain gelding; half-brother to 5f/6f winner Bankrupt (RPR 73); dam, unplaced over 6f-1m4f (RPR 44), is a sister to 1m4f Listed winner Crosspeace; the market can guide for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Phoenix Of Spain gelding; the market can guide for this newcomer

runners.name: Victorian Rose
runners.spotlight: Victor Ludorum filly; dam German 2yo/French 7.5f-1m3f winner (RPR 93); the betting should prove an accurate indicator for this newcomer.
runners.comment: Victor Ludorum filly; the betting should prove an accurate indicator for this newcomer

runners.name: Avispado
runners.spotlight: Well held in a pair of novices, hood left off when beating one home at Southwell (1m) last month; lots more is required.
runners.comment: Well held in a pair of novices, hood left off when beating one at Southwell last month

runners.name: Katie's Song
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when a debut fifth in 7f Southwell novice in April but beat just two home in C&D maiden two weeks ago; this Sergei Prokofiev filly shouldn't be written off.
runners.comment: Promise when 5th at Southwell but only beat two over C&D two weeks ago; not written off

races.race_name: Unibet 40,000+ Live Streamed Events Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Roserunner
runners.spotlight: Did well in his early starts on the AW during the winter including winning a Lingfield novice (1m, Polytrack); has regressed in his last two starts, albeit 1m2f may have been too far on turf debut at Yarmouth (good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) last month; needs to resume his progress.
runners.comment: Lingfield novice winner but has regressed in his last two starts; needs to resume progress

runners.name: Aneirin's Sword
runners.spotlight: Consistent after being switched to AW handicaps in January, including wins at Chelmsford and Wolverhampton; not at his best at Southwell last time and needs to bounce back; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Consistent after being switched to AW handicaps in January but not at his best last time

runners.name: Karakula Dancer
runners.spotlight: Most consistent since returning in January, winning three times on the AW including over C&D; another 3lb rise not the end of the world, but the outside stall may make things tricky.
runners.comment: Most consistent since returning in January, including a win over C&D; drawn widest, though

runners.name: Feisty Minx
runners.spotlight: Showed improvement when beaten just over a length into fourth of seven on handicap debut here (7f) 16 days ago; needs to step up again, but the extra furlong should suit so that is quite possible.
runners.comment: Fourth on handicap debut here last time; improvement needed, but extra furlong should suit
runners.owner: Mrs F Cotton, Mr & Mrs P Conway

runners.name: Fizzy Bear
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in three starts over 1m/8.5f on turf last month; open to improvement on handicap/AW debut and market support would be interesting.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in three starts on turf; open to improvement on handicap/AW debut

runners.name: Blue Celestial
runners.spotlight: In the frame in four of her six starts including finishing third in both outings here (7f); probably stays this far and each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Third in both outings here (7f); probably stays this far and each-way claims again

runners.name: Denby's Dream
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but running well lately, finishing runner-up at Lingfield (1m, Polytrack) last month and going down by a neck at Bath (1m, firm) 13 days later; races off the same mark and high on list.
runners.comment: Runner-up in last two starts; races off the same mark and high on list

runners.name: Kingofthecarnival
runners.spotlight: Returned from five months off to make a successful handicap debut at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta) in March, but hasn't built on it albeit not disgraced when fourth of 12 here (7f) last month; return to this trip not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Won on handicap debut at Newcastle in March, but not built on it; return to 1m a question

runners.name: Quick Turn
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) in March (a couple of those behind won their next starts); up 6lb after a short break but still one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Wolverhampton in March; up 6lb but still one for the shortlist

runners.name: Ziggy's Avenger
runners.spotlight: Hasn't beaten many in three starts over 1m on turf/Tapeta and has been gelded since last seen, but open to improvement on handicap debut and Oisin Murphy an eye-catching booking.
runners.comment: Hasn't beaten many in three starts, but been gelded; open to improvement on handicap debut
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing & Harry Kassap Llc

runners.name: Hakin Adraar
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in his first two starts over 7f on the AW for Alice Haynes last summer, but ran poorly in his last two outings; makes his stable debut after 228 days off having been gelded in the meantime; tongue-tie removed after one use; market useful.
runners.comment: Makes stable debut after 228 days off having been gelded in the meantime; market useful

runners.name: Suggy
runners.spotlight: Showed improvement when switched to handicaps in the autumn, finishing runner up at Bath (1m, soft) before going down by a head over this C&D; up 4lb on this return from six months off, but a few winners have come out of that race; respected.
runners.comment: Beaten a head over C&D in November and a few winners have come out of that race; respected

runners.name: Little Chief
runners.spotlight: Beaten upwards of 11l in three starts on turf (6f/6.5f) last summer; longer trip shouldn't be an issue on breeding and worth a market check on handicap/AW debut after nine months off.
runners.comment: Beaten upwards of 11l in three starts on turf last summer; worth a market check

runners.name: We Ride At Dawn
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in three maidens for Dominic French Davis last autumn, but far from disgraced when fifth of ten over 7f here (300-1) on final outing; open to improvement now handicapping after seven months off, but not ideally drawn; trainer's first runner (same owner).
runners.comment: Open to improvement now handicapping after seven months off, but not ideally drawn

races.race_name: Unibet More Extra Place Races Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Dannick
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden who can miss the break and he returned after wind surgery with a laboured effort over C&D (17-2) two weeks ago; finished behind three of today's rivals in that race and he needs to leave that form behind.
runners.comment: 13-race maiden who returned after wind surgery with a laboured effort over C&D 16 days ago

runners.name: Lady Manzor
runners.spotlight: Triple Polytrack winner who has been running well this spring and was only beaten around 2l on turf at Yarmouth (7f, good) last time; continues to edge down the weights and she should go well again back up in trip.
runners.comment: Triple Polytrack winner who has been in good form this spring; in the mix back up in trip

runners.name: American State
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent gelding who finished out the back at Wetherby last time, but he's won twice on AW this year including at Southwell (1m) on his penultimate run; hit a personal best with that comfortable success and has claims if he can recapture that form.
runners.comment: Hard to predict but he's won twice on AW this year and still looks on a workable mark

runners.name: Man Of Desert
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden who has form figures of 048705 in handicaps and he finished behind two of today's rivals over C&D two weeks ago; continues to drop down the weights but he needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden who has form figures of 048705 in handicaps; others preferred

runners.name: Influential
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden but he's finished runner-up twice including in a C&D handicap (5-1) two weeks ago; looks interesting off only 1lb higher and he still has potential as a handicapper.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who was runner-up in a C&D handicap two weeks ago; interesting contender

runners.name: Somkiyr
runners.spotlight: 9.5f winner in France as a 2yo, but he's been poor over hurdles for current yard and was well held back on the Flat here (1m3f) 16 days ago; overall record is now 1-14 and he needs to turn things around back in trip.
runners.comment: Has been poor over hurdles for current yard and was well held back on the Flat here latest

runners.name: Freda
runners.spotlight: 4yo who made it fourth-time lucky when landing a C&D novice last month; that was at 22-1 but she was strong at the finish and this well-bred filly is open to more progress on handicap debut; respected.
runners.comment: Won a C&D novice last month and she's open to more progress on handicap debut; respected

runners.name: Sant Alessio
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who won two handicaps in the second half of last year, including at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in November; that was in a tight four-way finish but he's only 2lb higher on his return and still has scope for further progress; needs a close look.
runners.comment: Ended last year with a win at Wolverhampton (8.6f) and he needs a close look on return

runners.name: Billy Mill
runners.spotlight: Eight-time AW winner who has finished placed over C&D in his last two starts; drawn out wide this time but he's tactically versatile and remains feasibly treated off his last winning mark; in the mix.
runners.comment: Eight-time AW winner who has finished placed over C&D in last two starts; in the mix

runners.name: Kaaranah
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a reduced mark when scoring at Wolverhampton (9.4f; first-time cheekpieces) last month and he followed up with a dominant display on his return visit two weeks later; 7lb higher here but he remains well handicapped on his old form including a win over C&D; key player.
runners.comment: Resurgent 9yo who has won at Wolverhampton in his last two starts; big player again

runners.name: Cephalus
runners.spotlight: 11-time AW winner but he's been out of sorts for new yard since December and was last of eight over C&D last time; mark is tumbling but he has lots to prove and needs a major revival.
runners.comment: 11-time AW winner but he's been out of sorts for current yard and needs a major revival

runners.name: Hour By Hour
runners.spotlight: All of his four wins have been at 6f on turf and last three were in 2024; well handicapped on old form but he was out of sorts in the autumn and has something to prove back up in trip for new yard (changed hands for 1,500gns).
runners.comment: All four wins have been at 6f and has something to prove back up in trip for new yard

runners.name: Bubbles Wonky
runners.spotlight: 2-17 on turf for Tim Easterby; well held switched to AW on first three runs for current yard but he raised his game with a solid third over C&D two weeks ago; ties in with Influential on that form and has claims if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Better effort when third over C&D latest and he ties in with Influential on that form

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Smartview Racecards Handicap

runners.name: I'm Workin On It
runners.spotlight: All of his four wins have been over C&D and latest was from the front in 14-runner race (7-2 favourite) two weeks ago; up 4lb but that was a personal best last time and he should make another bold bid; respected.
runners.comment: All four wins have been over C&D and latest was two weeks ago; respected

runners.name: Port Road
runners.spotlight: Improver who has won three of his last seven starts including when back from a break at Lingfield (7f, Polytrack) last month; up 4lb for that narrow success but he still looks feasibly treated and his first two wins were at this track; respected.
runners.comment: Improver who beat a clear second at Lingfield last time; respected up 4lb

runners.name: Melvin Udall
runners.spotlight: Two wins from six starts for three different yards but he was disappointing in a 7f handicap at Thirsk (ninth of 13) last month; has bit to prove again after that and he's untried on AW.
runners.comment: Two wins from six starts for three different yards but he was disappointing last time
runners.owner: K Mills, J Brown, S Barley

runners.name: Sea Suite
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-11 but his win came at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) in March and he was a close third at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last time; 3lb higher here but he still looks on a workable mark and has claims back in trip; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Won at Southwell in March and was close third at Wetherby latest; dangerous back in trip

runners.name: Rock Iguana
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who won a Southwell maiden (6f, Tapeta) last summer and was runner-up in a 7f handicap there last month; didn't get the best of runs in that race and that was a promising effort after five months off; interesting contender off unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who was runner-up on Southwell reappearance last month; interesting

runners.name: Farasi Lane
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner who scored twice at Wolverhampton (7f/8.6f) in March; only 1lb higher than for his last win and he's had excuses with traffic issues and a slow start in last two runs; should get a good tow into this race and has possibilities if he gets the breaks; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Dual course winner who has had excuses in last two runs; dangerous if he gets some luck

runners.name: Bell Shot
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner (6f-7.4f), who defied this mark at Southwell in January but he's been well held in three runs since; now drops back in grade but he needs to get back near his best.
runners.comment: On last winning mark but he needs to rediscover his spark on this drop back in grade
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Brunel Nation
runners.spotlight: Went close behind Tiger Crusade at Lingfield (7f) in January but he's been a long way below that form in three runs since; has plenty to prove again and he needs another bolt from the blue.
runners.comment: Went close at Lingfield in January but he's been back in the doldrums since
runners.owner: R Lane, L Watson & J Langridge

runners.name: Tiger Crusade
runners.spotlight: Ten-time winner who scored at Lingfield (7f, Polytrack) in Janaury but he's not got involved in his last three starts; now 2lb lower than for that last success but he needs to rediscover his spark.
runners.comment: His last win was off 2lb higher in January but he needs to rediscover his spark

runners.name: Al Karrar
runners.spotlight: Won on Windsor debut  in June 2022 and was runner-up in the Group 2 Richmond (6f, good to firm) next time; looks interesting on that form but he's had only three runs since and was well held in two handicaps last year; has bit to prove after another absence and this is a new trip.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo who has something to prove after another absence and this is a new trip

runners.name: Noodle Mission
runners.spotlight: Has claims on his best form last year but he ended last season with a poor run at Leicester (7f, good) in October and his last win was 21 months ago; others preferred.
runners.comment: On workable mark but he had a patchy record last season and his last win was 21 months ago

runners.name: Chalk Mountain
runners.spotlight: Six AW wins for Stuart Kittow including over C&D in his final run last season; returns for Zoe Hawkins after 238 days off but he's only 3lb higher than last time and has claims if he can pick up where he left off; market should be useful.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a C&D win and he needs watching in the market on his return

races.race_name: Try Unibet's New Improved Acca Boosts Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Amazonian Dream
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won for a while but was an encouraging third on his return at action at Windsor (6f, good to firm) 16 days ago; sports a first-time visor and is well-handicapped on his old form.
runners.comment: Was an encouraging third on his return at action at Windsor 16 days ago.

runners.name: Hockney
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 6f last September; below that level since returning this year and still 6lb higher than his last winning mark.
runners.comment: Won twice over 6f last September; below that level since returning this year.

runners.name: Hunky Dory
runners.spotlight: Both wins have been on AW over 6f, latest at Wolverhampton last month on his second start for this stable; flopped when favourite on his return to turf at Bath (5.5f, good) a week later and may be worth another chance back on AW.
runners.comment: Both wins have been on AW over 6f, latest at Wolverhampton last month.

runners.name: Irish Nectar
runners.spotlight: All three wins on turf when trained by Kevin Ryan; beaten a head over C&D in February but below that level twice since; may do better back at this track.
runners.comment: Only beaten a head over C&D in February but below that level twice since.

runners.name: Charlie Mason
runners.spotlight: Usually a reliable sort whose five wins have all been over 6f; beaten a head over 5f at Wolverhampton in March and a shade disappointing back on turf subsequently but should give a better performance here.
runners.comment: Usually a reliable sort whose five wins have all been over 6f; should run well.

runners.name: Havana Blast
runners.spotlight: Sole success came at Chelmsford AW in August 2024 and missed all of last year; showed he was on the way back when runner-up to an improver at Wolverhampton (5f) last month; steps up in trip and should run well.
runners.comment: Showed he was on the way back when runner-up to an improver at Wolverhampton last month.

runners.name: Many A Star
runners.spotlight: Veteran who showed his best form for a while when winning at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in February; below his best on turf at Goodwood 19 days ago but cannot be overlooked back on AW.
runners.comment: Below his best on turf at Goodwood 19 days ago but cannot be overlooked back on AW.

runners.name: Tan Rapido
runners.spotlight: Scored off this mark at Chelmsford (5f) last July; should be sharper following a run at Newmarket last month and steps back up to 6f; not out of this.
runners.comment: Should be sharper following a run at Newmarket last month and steps back up to 6f.

runners.name: Hierarchy
runners.spotlight: Has five wins on AW, including one over C&D; below his best since winning at Lingfield in February and best watched.
runners.comment: Below his best since winning at Lingfield in February and best watched.

runners.name: Moving Force
runners.spotlight: A fairly useful 5f juvenile winner for Richard Fahey; last of nine at Goodwood (6f, good) 19 days ago on stable debut following lengthy absence; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Last of nine at Goodwood 19 days ago on stable debut following lengthy absence.

runners.name: Change Sings
runners.spotlight: Has five wins, four being on AW but losing run stretches back to May 2024; gets little respite from the handicapper but has run with credit on all three starts this year.
runners.comment: Has five wins, four being on AW but losing run stretches back to May 2024.

runners.name: Divot
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden but he's returned from a break with two placed efforts in 1m4f handicaps (AW) this spring; on same mark at for his third at Lingfield last time and he's a big player if he responds well to first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 0-14 but he's been placed in both runs this spring and he's a key player; headgear added

runners.name: Maywedance
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in November but that is her sole win from 20 starts and she's been out of sort in four runs for current yard; continues to drop down the weights but she needs a major revival; cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: C&D winner but overall record is 1-20 and she's been out of sorts for current yard

runners.name: Marinakis
runners.spotlight: Gained all three wins for Ian Williams at classified level on AW; always behind on stable debut at Bath (11.6f, firm) last month but he now returns to Polytrack and has claims if he can get back near best; hood is removed and tongue-tie returns.
runners.comment: All three wins have been on AW and has claims if he can revive back on Polytrack

runners.name: Argentum
runners.spotlight: Creditable third over C&D in January but she's struggled since, including over hurdles in March; record is now 0-12 and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D in March but she's struggled since and record is now 0-12

runners.name: Dotties Star
runners.spotlight: Better known as a hurdler these days, but he made a successful return to the Flat when narrowly landing a classified event at Lingfield (1m4f, Polytrack) in March; never involved in a C&D handicap last time but that was against a pace bias and he has claims if things go more to plan this time.
runners.comment: Won at Lingfield on penultimate run and had excuse against pace bias last time; dangerous

runners.name: Voix De Bocelli
runners.spotlight: Placed four times on Polytrack (1m3f/1m4f) this year but he's struggled over hurdles and on the Flat in last two starts; has something to prove again and overall record is now 2-38.
runners.comment: Overall record of 2-38 and he's struggled over hurdles and on the Flat in last two runs

runners.name: Pablo Prince
runners.spotlight: His last win was in September 2024 but he went close at Chelmsford (1m2f) in February and he ran respectably over C&D last time; usually comes from off the pace and could be dangerous if he gets the breaks.
runners.comment: On lengthy losing run but he could be dangerous if this sets up for his closing style

runners.name: Uzincso
runners.spotlight: Seven of his eight wins have come at Kempton including over 1m3f in September; only 1lb higher than for that success but he's been disappointing since and was out the back on his C&D reappearance last month; down the list.
runners.comment: Seven-time course winner but he's been out of sorts in last four runs; down the list

runners.name: Big Take
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much in three AW runs at up to 1m4f for Hugo Palmer early last year; now goes handicapping after a gelding operation but he changed hands for just 1,000gns and needs improvement after a long absence.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but he returns after a long absence and needs improvement on handicap debut

runners.name: Master Dancer
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner but he's struggled at massive prices in three Flat runs (1m/1m2f) on Polytrack; now switches to a low-grade handicap but he needs a transformation on this step up to 1m4f.
runners.comment: Hurdle/chase winner but he's struggled in three Flat runs and needs a transformation

runners.name: Lawn Ranger
runners.spotlight: Veteran who is often a front-runner but he's not won since August 2023 and he's been well beaten in his two starts this year; on career-low mark but he has a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Veteran who has not won since August 2023 and he's struggled in both runs this year

runners.name: Taihang Scenery
runners.spotlight: 0-17, but 6lb lower than when runner-up on his return from seven months off over C&D in February and better drawn than when fourth in his last two starts here; worth a second look.
runners.comment: 0-17, but on a good mark based on his best form and better drawn than of late

runners.name: Spicy Spangle
runners.spotlight: 2-29 including a win over C&D; had dropped a long way in the weights when third over C&D last month and latest effort on dirt can be forgiven; down another 2lb and shortlisted.
runners.comment: Good third over C&D on latest turf run; down another 2lb and shortlisted

runners.name: Brilliant Fire
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all 15 starts despite a sliding mark and poorly drawn to boot; makes little appeal.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all 15 starts despite a sliding mark and poorly drawn to boot

runners.name: King Alloy
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is 0-15, but has made the frame in six of his nine starts this season including over C&D in March; hasn't enjoyed much luck with the draw lately, but no such excuses this time; high on list.
runners.comment: 0-15 but mostly running well this season; has the plum draw for a front-runner
runners.owner: Lun Ka Leung & Yeung Hiu Yu

runners.name: Circuit Victory
runners.spotlight: 1-21; won a dirt trial last month, but was again held back in a race at Sha Tin (6f) 17 days ago; has enough to prove back over this trip.
runners.comment: 1-21; has enough to prove back over this trip

runners.name: Island Breezes
runners.spotlight: Ran well in three starts over C&D around the turn of the year, but has regressed since and is now 0-18; again hasn't enjoyed much luck with the draw.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D around the turn of the year, but has regressed since and is now 0-18

runners.name: Sportic Warrior
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has shown virtually nothing in 13 starts; would be 6lb out of the weights if getting a run.
runners.comment: Has shown virtually nothing in 13 starts; would be 6lb out of the weights if getting a run
runners.owner: Edmund Wong Yan Ming, Michael Lee To Ming Et Al

runners.name: Country Dancer
runners.spotlight: 2-28 including a win over C&D; out of the frame in both starts since returning to Class 5 company, but he wasn't beaten far here five weeks ago and has dropped to a favourable mark; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Not beaten far here five weeks ago and has dropped to a favourable mark; each-way claims

runners.name: Majestic Delight
runners.spotlight: 1-19, the win coming at Sha Tin (6f) in March; looks opposable over this trip.
runners.comment: 1-19, the win coming at Sha Tin (6f) in March; looks opposable over this trip

runners.name: Flying Amani
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has shown some ability in eight starts over 6f-1m at Sha Tin; unraced over 5f, but would be dropping in grade with a new headgear combination if getting a run.
runners.comment: 0-8; would be dropping in grade with a new headgear combination if getting a run

runners.name: Young Striker
runners.spotlight: No closer than eighth in seven starts over 5f-7f on turf/dirt; off four months and his two barrier trials since last seen in a race make him far from compelling.
runners.comment: 0-7; two barrier trials since last seen in a race make him far from compelling

runners.name: Conspirator
runners.spotlight: 1-2 in Australia, but placed just once in 14 local starts; drop to Class 5 and his good draw are the positives, but he does need to show a bit more.
runners.comment: 0-14 in Hong Kong; drop to Class 5 and his good draw are the positives
runners.owner: Ng Sun Wah, Au Yau Lin & Alan Leung Wai Zen

runners.name: Heroic Master
runners.spotlight: 2-41 in Hong Kong, both wins coming over C&D; no success since October 2023, but still makes plenty of appeal down to this level for the first time and he has a good draw.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; down to this level for the first time and he has a good draw

runners.name: Handsome Blond
runners.spotlight: Very little to get excited about in his eight starts; off six months and although he has shown a little bit in his subsequent barrier trials, he still has plenty to prove over this trip from the outside stall.
runners.comment: 0-8; has plenty to prove over this trip from the outside stall

runners.name: Very Grateful
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in New Zealand but unplaced in eight starts in Hong Kong; enough to prove attempting this far for the first time.
runners.comment: Unplaced in eight starts in Hong Kong; enough to prove trying this far for the first time

runners.name: Aestheticism
runners.spotlight: Latest success came in a Class 3 over C&D just over a year ago, but 0-15 since; 6lb lower than when fourth here in March and well drawn, but his other recent efforts don't make him the percentage call.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 15; well drawn but still not the percentage call

runners.name: Vivacious Win
runners.spotlight: 0-10, but has a consistent record over C&D (422425); likely to figure again, especially with Zac Purton taking the ride.
runners.comment: 0-10, but has a consistent record over C&D (422425); likely to figure again

runners.name: Daily Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Well held in two starts since a promising debut fourth at Sha Tin (6f) in March, though did win a 5f trial in between; still unexposed but does have stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Well held in two official starts since a promising debut fourth; has stamina to prove
runners.owner: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho

runners.name: Turin Warrior
runners.spotlight: All four local wins have come over C&D and he's back off the same mark as for the latest in November; stall 11 puts a dampener on things, though.
runners.comment: All four local wins over C&D and he's back on last winning mark, but stall 11 not ideal

runners.name: Bull Attitude
runners.spotlight: Running consistently well on dirt lately, but just 1-19 and a bit more will be needed if he is to double his tally.
runners.comment: Just 1-19 and a bit more will be needed if he is to double his tally

runners.name: To Infinity
runners.spotlight: All seven wins over C&D, the latest last September; now 5lb lower, but not firing of late and has the outside stall; blinkers removed.
runners.comment: All seven wins over C&D, but not firing of late and has the outside stall
runners.owner: Chan Wing Chiu & Yildiz Choi Yat Fan

runners.name: Ivy League
runners.spotlight: All three local wins have come over C&D, the latest a year ago when the drop to Class 4 company did the trick; off four months, but has shaped with promise in his last two trials and is a major player back at this level.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner, the latest when dropped to Class 4; major player back at this level

runners.name: Never Too Soon
runners.spotlight: Much better known as a sprinter, but was only beaten a short head and a head into third over C&D three weeks ago; his wide draw isn't ideal, though.
runners.comment: Best known as a sprinter, but a close third over C&D three weeks ago; wide draw not ideal

runners.name: The Absolute
runners.spotlight: His best form, including both wins, has come over 7f at Sha Tin; finished behind two of these over C&D three weeks ago and others make greater appeal despite his good draw.
runners.comment: Best form has come over 7f at Sha Tin; behind two of these over C&D three weeks ago

runners.name: Magic Super
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has shown little in six starts over 5f-7f; will need to improve plenty if getting a run and will also have stamina to prove; eyeshield removed after one use.
runners.comment: Has shown little in six starts over 5f-7f; will need to improve plenty

runners.name: Beauty Gemini
runners.spotlight: Winner of three barrier trials, but well beaten in three races over 5f/6f at Sha Tin; has plenty to prove, not least at the trip.
runners.comment: Winner of three barrier trials, but well beaten in three races at Sha Tin; plenty to prove
runners.owner: Yuki Kong Yuk Ki & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Glorious Journey
runners.spotlight: Patchy form since gaining his second C&D success in November and now 10lb higher, but was only just beaten in two of his last three starts and wouldn't be far away from a good draw if producing his best.
runners.comment: 10lb above last winning mark, but only just beaten in two of his last three starts

runners.name: The Cosmic Power
runners.spotlight: Reserve; his two efforts over 6f at Sha Tin leave plenty to be desired.
runners.comment: His two efforts over 6f at Sha Tin leave plenty to be desired
runners.owner: Lam King Wai, Dr Lam Kai Cheong Et Al

runners.name: Legend Star
runners.spotlight: Two wins over C&D this season; not the most consistent, but he has the plum draw so a big effort wouldn't be a surprise.
runners.comment: Not the most consistent, but he has the plum draw so a big effort wouldn't be a surprise

runners.name: Brownneedsfurther
runners.spotlight: 0-10; mainly seen at Sha Tin, but has been placed in all three starts over C&D; frame material again with a first-time visor replacing blinkers.
runners.comment: 0-10, but placed in all three starts over C&D; frame material again; visor on

runners.name: Live Wire
runners.spotlight: In the frame in three of his five starts over 5f/6f at Sha Tin; Zac Purton keeps the faith, but he doesn't have a great draw for his first visit here.
runners.comment: In the frame in three of his five starts at Sha Tin; doesn't have a great draw

runners.name: Free Pony
runners.spotlight: Still just 1lb higher than when winning over C&D in December and has run well in both starts since returned here in March; respected.
runners.comment: 1lb higher than when winning over C&D in December and has run well in his last two starts
runners.owner: Edwin Fong Yick Chung, Edmund Ho Kwok Shing & So Wai Keung

runners.name: Stormy Knight
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in five starts over 6f at Sha Tin; third in a dirt trial since last seen in a race, but still some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in five starts over 6f at Sha Tin; some way down the pecking order
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs James Tien Pei Chun

runners.name: Genius Baby
runners.spotlight: Reserve; still 5lb higher than when winning over C&D in December, but has made the frame off higher marks on a few occasions since; each-way claims again if getting in.
runners.comment: 5lb higher than when winning over C&D in December, but has since run well off higher marks
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Pang Wai Hak, Frankie Pang Ka Yeung & A Pang Ka Wai

runners.name: Georgian Sigma
runners.spotlight: In the frame all three starts over 5f/6f at Sha Tin; better drawn this time and a player if as effective around here.
runners.comment: In the frame in all three starts at Sha Tin; a player if as effective around here
runners.owner: Clement Cheng Ka Man, Alfred Choi & Roger Li Ka Chun

runners.name: Cheaha
runners.spotlight: 1-4 in New Zealand and 0-9 in Hong Kong; beaten a fair way into fourth when dropped to this level over C&D three weeks ago, but is much better drawn this time; could go well.
runners.comment: Beaten a fair way into fourth over C&D three weeks ago, but is much better drawn this time

runners.name: Captain Link
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in Hong Kong, but was transformed by the switch to forcing tactics when beaten a short head over C&D four weeks ago and Joao Moreira is booked again; one to consider.
runners.comment: 0-6, but only beaten a short head over C&D four weeks ago; could go well

runners.name: Copartner Fleet
runners.spotlight: Not the best of draws, but drops back into a Class 4 for the first time since winning here (5f) in January; could play a part.
runners.comment: Drops back to Class 4 for the first time since winning here in January; could play a part

runners.name: Vulcanus
runners.spotlight: Well drawn and still 1lb below his last winning mark, but he has a better record on dirt (3-16) than on turf (1-25); may again find one or two too good.
runners.comment: Has a better record on dirt (3-16) than on turf (1-25); may again find one or two too good

runners.name: Heroic Vanguard
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 6f at Sha Tin, his first run much better than his second; best watched from the widest stall.
runners.comment: Both starts at Sha Tin, the first run much better than the second; drawn widest

runners.name: Vigor Elleegant
runners.spotlight: 1-19, the win coming in a Class 5 over C&D a year ago; only beaten just over a length into third in a Class 4 here three weeks ago, though, and although not as well drawn this time he still has to be considered.
runners.comment: Won over C&D a year ago and best effort since when third three weeks ago; considered

runners.name: Sure Joyful
runners.spotlight: Lost his way after winning over C&D a year ago but, having dropped in the weights, he was only beaten a short head and a nose into third here four weeks ago; widest draw not welcome, though.
runners.comment: Much better effort last time and still well handicapped, but widest draw not welcome
runners.owner: Star Chan Iu Seng, Simon Chan Cham Man Et Al

runners.name: Another Zonda
runners.spotlight: Narrowly gained his second C&D success three weeks ago; 5lb higher, but remains in Class 4 company and one for the shortlist despite his wide draw.
runners.comment: Gained his second C&D success three weeks ago; 5lb higher, but remains in Class 4 company

runners.name: Decision Link
runners.spotlight: 0-10 at Sha Tin but 1-1 here, albeit the win came over 6f last month; better drawn than of late, but he still needs to prove his stamina.
runners.comment: 1-1 here, albeit the win came over 6f last month; needs to prove his stamina
runners.owner: Mixion Lam Ting Kwok & Luo Hong Fen

runners.name: Luck Is Back
runners.spotlight: 4-4 in Brazil but 0-17 in Hong Kong; beaten less than a length into third over 1m1f here in December, but twice well held since and the application of an eyeshield made little difference on stable debut last time; best watched for now.
runners.comment: 4-4 in Brazil but 0-17 in Hong Kong; best watched for now

runners.name: Our Lucky Glory
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 6f here; losing run up to 22, but his record over C&D during the past year reads 43043334 so not ruled out each-way; first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Record over C&D during past year reads 43043334 so not ruled out each-way; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Leaping Star
runners.spotlight: 2-8 when trained in Ireland, but has finished well down the field in all ten Hong Kong starts; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Well down the field in all ten Hong Kong starts; difficult to enthuse over
runners.owner: Maurice Lai Tai Hei, John Lo Et Al

runners.name: Blazing Beam
runners.spotlight: Won a Class 5 over 6f here in January, but well held in four starts at this level since; has stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Won a Class 5 over 6f here in January, but well held in four starts at this level since
runners.owner: Roger Chan Tse Man & Bosco Chan

runners.name: Northern Beast
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over 1m/1m1f here; 12lb lower than when last successful, but has become inconsistent; first-time cheekpieces given a go.
runners.comment: 12lb lower than when last successful, but has become inconsistent; cheekpieces on
runners.owner: Ngai Man, Amy Wang Hong & Doris Ngai Yan Tung

runners.name: Fortune Star
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in five starts since winning over C&D in January, but continues to edge down the weights and now just 2lb higher than for that success; not the best of draws, but a big player nonetheless.
runners.comment: Dropping in the weights and just 2lb higher than when winning over C&D in January; player
runners.owner: Kelvin Chan Kam Kei & Eddie Chan Kam Kong

runners.name: Daily Trophy
runners.spotlight: Reserve; best efforts, including all four wins, have come over 6f on dirt at Sha Tin; opposable under these conditions.
runners.comment: Best efforts have come over 6f on dirt at Sha Tin; opposable under these conditions
runners.owner: Peter Cheng & Jerry Cheng Chun Ho

runners.name: Colourful Gan
runners.spotlight: 2-4 in Australia, but no closer than ninth in seven starts on turf/dirt in Hong Kong; needs a good deal more.
runners.comment: No closer than ninth in seven starts on turf/dirt in Hong Kong; needs a good deal more
runners.owner: Gan Wee Sean, Gary Gan Cheng Chuah & Gavin Gan Cheng Hooi

runners.name: Flying Fortune
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in Australia, but out of the frame in five starts over 6f/7f at Sha Tin; down another 2lb, but needs plenty more.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in five starts at Sha Tin; down another 2lb, but needs plenty more

runners.name: Lucky Together
runners.spotlight: 1-2 in Australia, but 0-7 in Hong Kong; drawn wide, but he had the same stall when beaten less than a length into fourth behind Another Zonda over C&D (first-time hood) three weeks ago; 5lb better off and respected.
runners.comment: Less than a length behind Another Zonda over C&D three weeks ago and now 5lb better off

races.race_name: The Ewo Challenge Trophy Handicap (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)

runners.name: Happy United
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 5f/6f here, but losing run up to 25; another placing may be his best hope.
runners.comment: Three wins over 5f/6f here, but losing run up to 25; another placing may be his best hope

runners.name: Harmony Fire
runners.spotlight: All five wins over C&D, the latest last September; again has enjoyed no luck with the draw, but that hasn't prevented him from running well lately and he could do so again.
runners.comment: All five wins over C&D; again not well drawn but could still run well

runners.name: Bits Superstar
runners.spotlight: 1-22 in Hong Kong, the win coming over 6f here a year ago; the return to 5f poses a question, but he does possess early speed and has the inside stall; could go well.
runners.comment: Return to 5f poses a question, but he does possess early speed and has the inside stall
runners.owner: Tam Jin Rong & Zhao Xiao Na

runners.name: Grand Nova
runners.spotlight: 2-22 including a win over C&D; running well in defeat here prior to a lesser effort at Sha Tin (5f) 11 days ago; frame material returned to this venue.
runners.comment: 2-22 including a win over C&D; frame material returned to this venue

runners.name: Super Sixty
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than when successful over C&D just over a year ago and 5lb lower than when runner-up over C&D in October; recent efforts don't suggest he is ready to take advantage, though.
runners.comment: On a favourable mark, but recent efforts don't suggest he is ready to take advantage

runners.name: Beauty Thunder
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Sha Tin (5f) 11 days ago, but looks better here with his record over C&D reading 4122224; drawn wider than ideal, but still holds each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Record over C&D reads 4122224; has a high draw, but still holds each-way claims at least

runners.name: Flying Ting Lok
runners.spotlight: 1-2 in Australia but unplaced in eight local starts; may appreciate the return to these conditions, but the draw hasn't been kind.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all eight local starts; the draw hasn't been kind

runners.name: Zephyr
runners.spotlight: Has not shown a massive amount in five starts over 6f; needs the drop in trip and blinkers to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Not shown much in five starts; needs drop in trip to have a positive effect; blinkers on

runners.name: Beauty Show
runners.spotlight: Beaten half a length by Tycoon Resources on his official debut over C&D last month and now 5lb better off; ran to a similar level when fourth back here a fortnight later and again shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Ran well in both starts over C&D last month and again shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Giant Spirit
runners.spotlight: 5-10 in Australia, but not really got going in four local starts; this is a drop in grade and he is well drawn, though, so not a great surprise were he to take a significant step forward.
runners.comment: Drops in grade and well drawn; not a great surprise if he takes a significant step forward
runners.owner: Dr V Chan Chi Tao, John Tang Lam Chek & Uri Yau Wing Hay

runners.name: Tycoon Resources
runners.spotlight: Had three of these behind when making a successful official debut over C&D six weeks ago, since when he has won his third trial; up 7lb, but still open to improvement and high on the list.
runners.comment: Made a successful debut over C&D six weeks ago; up 7lb, but still open to improvement

runners.name: Boltz
runners.spotlight: Reserve; made all in a barrier trial over C&D this month; interesting if able to make his official debut with a tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Made all in a barrier trial over C&D this month; tongue-tie on for debut

runners.name: Winning Money
runners.spotlight: Again drawn wide, though he did defy the outside stall over 6f here in January; twice held off this 5lb higher mark since, though, and another furlong would probably have suited.
runners.comment: Held off this mark in his last two starts and another furlong would probably have suited

runners.name: Double Alpha
runners.spotlight: Reserve; made all in a 4f barrier trial this month; worth a second look if getting a run.
runners.comment: Made all in a 4f barrier trial this month; worth a second look

runners.name: The Heir
runners.spotlight: Placed four times in a row over C&D in February-April and didn't run badly when fifth at Sha Tin last time; 2lb lower today and, from stall 4, is likely to make his presence felt under Joao Moreira.
runners.comment: Very consistent 6yo; aided by recent 2lb drop and favourable draw today; likely contender

runners.name: Thunder Prince
runners.spotlight: Belatedly off the mark in Hong Kong with comfortable C&D win last month, his 22nd attempt; had to settle for fourth off new mark three weeks ago, but has the inside stall this time and Zac Purton keeps the ride; major player.
runners.comment: C&D winner last month; only fourth off new mark since but has plum inside draw this time
runners.owner: Wilson Chu Kwok Yiu, Paul M F Cheng Et Al

runners.name: Everstar
runners.spotlight: Won twice over C&D last year but has been in the doldrums for a good while now; revival needed.
runners.comment: Won twice over C&D last year but has been in the doldrums for a good while now
runners.owner: Jackie F C Hang, Colin L P Cheung & Fredrick F C Lun

runners.name: Fatal Blow
runners.spotlight: Kept on for fifth over C&D last month and continues to edge down the weights, but remains without a win since April 2025 and others in this line-up have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Ran okay over C&D last month but this steadily regressive 5yo has not win since April 2025

runners.name: Superb King
runners.spotlight: 0-12 in Hong Kong but placed here (5f-6f) on last three appearances; made bold bid from the front when second three weeks ago and is well drawn if similar tactics are planned.
runners.comment: 0-12 in Hong Kong but placed here (5f-6f) on last three outings and has good draw

runners.name: Falcon Hunter
runners.spotlight: Did well to finish third over C&D at triple-digit odds in February, his fifth start, but did not get competitive here last month; well drawn, but not easy to predict in first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Placed at big odds over C&D in February but did not get competitive here last time

runners.name: Meowth
runners.spotlight: In the frame five consecutive times over C&D since January, the first three in Class 3; recent 2lb drop is helpful but a double-digit draw isn't.
runners.comment: In the frame five consecutive times over C&D but wide draw no help today

runners.name: Argento Ocean
runners.spotlight: Came good at fifth attempt, with C&D win in November, and has remained in good form since; runner-up off today's mark over C&D last month but dealt duff hand draw-wise today.
runners.comment: Has remained in good form since C&D win in November but dealt duff hand-draw-wise today

runners.name: Yeux De Lifeline
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all five starts, most recently when only eighth from plum inside stall here last month; probably needs a drop in grade.
runners.comment: Unplaced all five starts, most recently when only eighth from the plum inside stall

runners.name: Starry Show
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced when midfield over C&D three weeks ago and continues to slip down the weights, but is yet to be placed after nine Hong Kong starts and is not drawn well.
runners.comment: Not disgraced over C&D three weeks ago but yet to be placed after nine Hong Kong starts

runners.name: Gor Gor
runners.spotlight: Has gone the wrong way since narrow C&D win in February and is still 5lb above that mark; makes very limited appeal from stall 11.
runners.comment: Has gone the wrong way since his narrow C&D win in February and is drawn wide

runners.name: Virtus Glory
runners.spotlight: Raced too freely on the outside of the field and was unable to justify prominence in the betting when only ninth on recent debut at Sha Tin, but has a better draw here; no surprise if he gets much closer to the target.
runners.comment: Raced too freely when poorly drawn on recent debut; can do better

runners.name: California Bay
runners.spotlight: Reserve; led close home to win on recent dirt debut at Sha Tin; evidently in good nick but has 0-16 strike-rate on turf in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Recently won on dirt debut but has 0-16 strike-rate on turf in Hong Kong

runners.name: Z Prince
runners.spotlight: Reserve; a big-priced outsider for last month's debut at Sha Tin (7f) but kept on steadily for very respectable fifth; now needs to build on that performance over this shorter trip.
runners.comment: Kept on steadily for respectable fifth on last month's debut at Sha Tin (7f)
runners.owner: Lee Ching Woon & Candy Chan Chor Tan

runners.name: Violet Star
runners.spotlight: Placed for first time in Hong Kong when running-on third over C&D last month, but took a backward step when down the field over 1m1f a fortnight later and is not easy to predict today.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D last month but latest run was a backward step; hard to predict

runners.name: Do Your Part
runners.spotlight: Consistent and steadily progressive 5yo who won at Sha Tin (7f) in November and has been placed on his other eight starts this season (6f-1m); drawn wide today but still respected.
runners.comment: Consistent and steadily progressive 5yo; drawn wide but still considered

runners.name: Win Method
runners.spotlight: Three wins this season, the last two in Class 4 C&D events in March; ran big race from the front when close third in this higher grade last month; must be considered in current form.
runners.comment: Won twice over C&D in March and was placed in this higher grade last month; respected

runners.name: Casa Rochester
