runners.spotlight: Began season with two good runs, the latter a C&D third in October, but this is his first run since and he's drawn on the outside.
runners.comment: Began season with two good runs but has wide draw on first start since October

runners.name: Another World
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped if judged on best of this season's dirt form, but finished last of 14 on turf on last two appearances (1m/7f).
runners.comment: Dirt winner this season but last of 14 on turf on last two appearances

runners.name: Power Of Vitam
runners.spotlight: Left low-key Hong Kong debut behind when causing 46-1 surprise at Sha Tin (1m) in February, but was not quite in the same form on either subsequent outing (1m/7f); needs to kick on again.
runners.comment: Surprise winner in February, his second HK start, but not quite in same form since

runners.name: California Moxie
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner in Class 4 this season but has found things tougher since upped to this grade, albeit he kept on well for fourth here three weeks ago.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner in Class 4 this season but has found things tougher since upped in grade

runners.name: Riding Together
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f in February and seemed to have emerged from a dip in form when fourth over that trip this month, but is not yet proven over this far and has a double-digit draw to contend with.
runners.comment: Back in good form over 7f this month but not yet proven over this far and has wide draw

runners.name: Lovero
runners.spotlight: In good form at Sha Tin for new stable this year, most recently runner-up there last month; well drawn here and likely to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: In good form for new stable this year and likely to be right in the thick of things again

runners.name: Magnemite
runners.spotlight: 7f winner when based in Australia; soundly beaten over 6f on first two Hong Kong starts and needs this longer trip to show him in a much better light.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten over 6f on first two Hong Kong starts; tries a new trip today

runners.name: Hott Shott
runners.spotlight: Useful over 7f/1m when based in Britain but has struggled in both Hong Kong starts (1m/6f).
runners.comment: Useful over 7f/1m when based in Britain but has struggled on both Hong Kong starts (1m/6f)

runners.name: Sky Vino
runners.spotlight: Scored by a neck on dirt at Sha Tin (1m) a fortnight ago, his second success on that surface this year, and is also suited by this C&D; up 6lb to another career-high mark but ought to give this a give shot from stall 1.
runners.comment: Dual dirt winner this year; also suited by this C&D; likely contender from stall 1

runners.name: Continent Express
runners.spotlight: Reserve; ended three-race career in New Zealand with 7f win in September; will be making Hong Kong debut if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: 7f winner in New Zealand in September; makes Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Titan Pegasus
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 2-3 as a 2yo in Australia, the latter win gained in 5.5f Group 3; makes Hong Kong debut after 452-day absence, though, and is entitled to benefit from this outing.
runners.comment: 2yo Group 3 winner when based in Australia; makes Hong Kong debut after long absence

runners.name: Sunny Da Best
runners.spotlight: Placed over 7f at Sha Tin two starts ago and kept on for respectable fifth over 6f there last time; another 2lb lower here and considered each-way.
runners.comment: In decent form at Sha Tin lately and continues to edge down the weights; each-way claims

runners.name: Blue Illusion
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; ran on strongly to cause 97-1 shock over 5f here in December, his third Hong Kong start, but five lesser efforts have followed and he can't be supported with much confidence on return to 6f.
runners.comment: Shock 5f course winner in December but five lesser efforts have followed

runners.name: Telecom Fighters
runners.spotlight: Looked out of sorts on his two 1m starts last month, but belied triple-digit odds when fourth from stall 11 over C&D a week ago; drawn even wider today, though.
runners.comment: Belied huge odds when fourth over C&D last week but is drawn even wider today

runners.name: Chateauneuf
runners.spotlight: Now 2lb lower than when winning over C&D in November, but has been badly out of form this year and his favourable draw is not enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Badly out of form this year; favourable draw not enough to tempt

runners.name: King Miles
runners.spotlight: Battled on well for narrow C&D win off today's mark in November, but subsequent form is uninspiring and he was particularly disappointing when last in action.
runners.comment: Narrow C&D winner in November but subsequent form has been uninspiring
runners.owner: Bernie Yim Mei Fung & Yim Man Hay

runners.name: Flying Wrote
runners.spotlight: Very consistent 5yo who probably posted a career-best performance when close second to Giant Ballon over C&D three weeks ago; 5lb better off with that rival today; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Career-best form when close second to Giant Ballon here last month; 5lb better off today
runners.owner: Simon Tsang Heung Tung & Shirley Tsang Huang Yang

runners.name: Grit Spirit
runners.spotlight: A well-beaten outsider on first two Hong Kong starts, both over 6f at Sha Tin, and has a tough draw here.
runners.comment: A well-beaten outsider on first two Hong Kong starts and has a tough draw here

runners.name: Storming Dragon
runners.spotlight: Well below best form this year but, from a much-reduced mark, did well to win Class 4 C&D contest from stall 11 last month; quickly back up in grade today but has much more favourable draw this time; a possible.
runners.comment: Did well to win from stall 11 last month, albeit off a much-reduced mark; back up in grade

runners.name: Sky Cap
runners.spotlight: Resurgent 4yo who won consecutive C&D starts in March/April and was placed over 5f when back in this higher grade four weeks ago; wide draw dents his appeal, though.
runners.comment: Third in this higher grade (5f) since winning twice over C&D this year; drawn wide, though

runners.name: Fiery Steed
runners.spotlight: Reserve; struggled to get competitive on first three Hong Kong starts (5f-6f); tongue-tie now added.
runners.comment: Struggled to get competitive on first three Hong Kong starts

runners.name: All Out For Six
runners.spotlight: Smart when based in South Africa and launched Hong Kong career with three decent efforts from double-digit stalls at Sha Tin (6f-7f) this year; has enjoyed better luck on the draw front here; each-way claims at least.
runners.comment: Launched HK career with three decent efforts from double-digit stalls; each-way claims
runners.owner: James Lau Po Man & Alice Woo Wai See

runners.name: A Americ Te Specso
runners.spotlight: Much better known as a miler nowadays and has been out of form over that trip lately.
runners.comment: Much better known as a miler nowadays and has been out of form over that trip lately

runners.name: Giant Ballon
runners.spotlight: Fast-improving 4yo who held on well from Flying Wrote to complete a C&D hat-trick three weeks ago; nicely drawn today and commands respect.
runners.comment: Fast-improving 4yo who held on well to complete a C&D hat-trick three weeks ago
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs George Chang And George Hing Chang

runners.name: Sugar Goodson
runners.spotlight: Reserve; caused 33-1 surprise in 1m Irish maiden on debut in October but this drop to 6f is not an obvious move on his Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: Won 1m maiden on debut in Ireland; drop to 6f not an obvious move on Hong Kong debut

runners.name: Glowing Praises
runners.spotlight: Made winning Hong Kong debut over 5f at Sha Tin in February and has been placed over that trip on his last three appearances, most recently when second from 5lb out of the weights here three weeks ago; effectively 5lb lower today and should be fine back at 6f; has a big role to play from stall 2.
runners.comment: Placed three times over 5f since winning HK debut over that trip in February; a player

runners.name: Rising Force
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo who has won twice at Sha Tin this year and lost out only by a nose there last month; obvious contender if that form can be transferred to this sharper track.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo who has won twice at Sha Tin this year; high on the list
runners.owner: Tai Woon Shing & Chan Koon Man

runners.name: Colourful King
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who recovered from slow start to take third behind Bottomuptogether over 5f here last month; deserted by Zac Purton today, but the move back up in trip will suit and his wide draw is not a huge inconvenience, given he's usually ridden very patiently.
runners.comment: Progressive 4yo; usually ridden patiently, so wide draw not a huge inconvenience

runners.name: Bottomuptogether
runners.spotlight: Back in good form since returning from a break, more recently when winning over 5f here three weeks ago; still on a workable mark after recent 5lb rise and this longer trip also suits.
runners.comment: Still on workable mark after recent 5f course win and this longer trip also suits

runners.name: Victor The Winner
runners.spotlight: 6f Group 1 winner in his prime but in sharp decline this season, albeit not beaten that far on last two appearances.
runners.comment: 6f Group 1 winner in his prime but in sharp decline this season; others preferred

runners.name: Sight Hermoso
runners.spotlight: Has taken his form to new heights in recent weeks, winning twice over C&D; up in grade today but not yet fully exposed after only ten starts and has a good draw; good chance.
runners.comment: Has taken his form to new heights in recent weeks, winning twice over C&D; good chance
runners.owner: Alvin Tam Chi Fung & Dr Andy Poon Tsz Chung

runners.name: Magic Control
runners.spotlight: Reserve; well handicapped on early-season form but just a respectable fifth here (5f) last month and evidently not firing on all cylinders.
runners.comment: Just a respectable fifth here (5f) last month and evidently not firing on all cylinders

runners.name: Emblazon
runners.spotlight: Won four consecutive handicaps (6f-7f) before running as though something was amiss in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) in March, but was again disappointing when back in a 7f handicap last month and this drop back to 6f is probably not ideal.
runners.comment: Has made very good progress this season but the wheels seems to have come off lately

runners.name: Turquoise Velocity
runners.spotlight: Progressive 4yo who scored on soft ground at Sha Tin (6f) this month and is now 4-6; still unexposed over this trip and probably still has more to offer but a wide draw asks a lot of him today.
runners.comment: Scored on soft ground this month and is now 4-6; wide draw makes this a test, though

runners.name: Regal Gem
runners.spotlight: Gained all five wins over 6f; placed over 1m in February and subsequent defeat in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) can easily be overlooked; returns to sprinting here; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Five-time 6f winner; easily excused defeat in HK Derby (1m2f); a possible on sprint return

runners.name: Geneva
runners.spotlight: Placed on consecutive starts before coming from off the pace for emphatic win at Sha Tin last month; latest 7lb rise enforces this rise in grade but he's evidently in prime form.
runners.comment: Came from off the pace for emphatic Sha Tin win last month; 7lb higher here; respected

runners.name: Storm Rider
runners.spotlight: Three wins this season, the most notable a last-gasp success in 7f Group 3 handicap in January; has the services of Zac Purton today and can cope with this drop back in trip, but wide draw makes things awkward.
runners.comment: Three wins this season, including a Group 3 handicap; wide draw makes things awkward

runners.name: Young Champion
runners.spotlight: Reserve; generally in very good form this year, but does not have much margin for error off current mark and needs to rebound quickly from a disappointing effort 11 days ago.
runners.comment: Largely in good form this year but didn't fire last time and remains on tough mark

runners.name: Sky Trust
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner at Sha Tin this season; bounced back from a below-par effort when fifth there last month and is well drawn here; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Sound effort when fifth at Sha Tin las month and well drawn here; probably in the mix
runners.owner: Bryan Kwan Hon Ming, Jessica Kwan Mun Hang Et Al
